Title: Manager, Data Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 259823

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Manager, Data Analyst will work with Canadian Wealth Management Data Operations to provide data-driven solutions to various Wealth business applications data. This position involves maintaining data extraction, transformation processes, and reporting development to support regulatory, compliance and ongoing business operations.

Is this role right for you? In this role you will:


Work closely with the Canadian Wealth Management Operations team to support day-to-day reporting activities
Deliver the daily scheduled reports and support Adhoc reporting requirements with complete data analysis and provide the technical solution to meet the customer requirements
Work with data investigation, analysis, develop and maintain a strong knowledge of wealth management business workflow to deliver the expected solution
As a Senior Business Analyst Specialist who ensures re-engineering, you will work with the business to understand existing functionalities, and develop new solutions, and write scripts for analysis, testing and regression, understanding relationship database structure and how the data maps to various business application
Identify programming / analysis skill gaps and keep up with the new data analysis tools and programming software to consistently improve self and existing processes.
Be responsible for performing heavy data-driven analytics and reconciliations for complex calculation logic in various wealth application data which is highly statistical and computation intensive


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? – We’d love to work with you if you have:


A University degree in quantitative discipline (mathematics, science, computer science, or engineering)
A minimum of 5 years of industry and business analysis experience
An understanding in wealth business management
Data Knowledge in wealth or financial related business
In-depth knowledge of relational database concepts
In-depth knowledge of Data analytics and Business intelligence
SQL, AS400, Microsoft Access, Microsoft VBA, or VB .Net, and Sybase
Experience using Power BI, Python, Databricks is an asset
Experience with GCP is an asset
Experience in AI is an asset
The ability to adapt to change quickly and adjust work accordingly in a positive manner
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail


Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Power Platform Data Analyst
Description:
English version below / Usage de l'anglais requis pour échanger avec collègues anglophones et relations d'affaires avec d'autres provinces / US.




Offre d’emploi – Power BI / Power Platform Analyst

Localisation : Montréal

Type de contrat : Permanent

Mode de travail : Hybride – 3 jours/semaine au bureau

Salaire : Jusqu’à 95 000 $

Le masculin est utilisé pour alléger l’annonce.




🚀 Ce que nous allons accomplir ensemble

Vous rejoignez une équipe technologique stratégique, au cœur des initiatives d’optimisation des processus, d’automatisation et de GenAI.

Votre impact sera concret : rendre la donnée exploitable, lisible et utile à l’échelle de l’entreprise grâce à des rapports fiables, harmonisés et orientés décision.




🎯 Votre mission (si vous l’acceptez !)

En tant que Power BI / Power Platform Analyst, vous jouerez un rôle clé dans la conception et l’évolution de solutions de reporting utilisées par des équipes multidisciplinaires, dans un environnement structuré et exigeant.




📅 Votre quotidien sera rythmé par :

Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord Power BI performants et intuitifs 📊
Traduire les besoins d’affaires en modèles de données clairs et exploitables
Développer et optimiser des solutions sur la Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse)
Rédiger une documentation solide : sources de données, règles de gestion, définitions
Collaborer avec des parties prenantes réparties sur plusieurs sites 🌍
Former et accompagner les utilisateurs finaux pour maximiser l’adoption
Améliorer en continu la qualité, la performance et la pertinence des rapports




👥 Votre future équipe

Vous évoluerez au sein de l’équipe Fleet Reporting, rattachée à la division Technology COO – Process Optimization, dans un contexte data-driven, enterprise et hautement collaboratif.




🧰 Votre stack de jeu

Power BI (Desktop & Service)
DAX & Power Query
Power Platform : Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse
SQL (extraction, transformation, modélisation)
Bonnes pratiques en BI & data modeling




🧠 Vous êtes la bonne personne si :

Vous avez 5+ ans d’expérience professionnelle, dont 3+ ans en BI / Data Analytics
Vous êtes hands-on et aimez être dans le concret (profil IC senior, non managérial)
Vous êtes à l’aise dans des environnements grandes entreprises / réglementés
Vous savez vulgariser la donnée et raconter une histoire claire avec des chiffres 📈
Vous aimez collaborer avec des interlocuteurs variés et documenter votre travail




🌟 Atouts appréciés

Expérience en finance ou services financiers
Intérêt pour les sujets GenAI & automatisation
Connaissance de Snowflake (Cortex = gros plus)




🎁 Les avantages Astek

Jusqu’à 4 semaines de congés + 5 jours mobiles
Assurances collectives complètes (télémédecine & PAE inclus)
Remboursement du transport en commun 🚇
Forfait cellulaire & matériel informatique 💻
Allocation sport / bien-être 🏃 ♀️
Programme de cooptation
Activités sociales & team buildings 🎉




🤝 Rencontrons-nous !

Vous vous reconnaissez dans ce rôle ?

Postulez directement et Sara, Spécialiste en Acquisition de Talents, vous contactera pour un premier échange.

Vous rencontrerez ensuite Floriane, votre futur manager, pour discuter du poste et de vos ambitions.




-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Job Offer – Power BI / Power Platform Analyst 📊

Location: Montreal

Contract: Permanent

Work model: Hybrid – 3 days/week in office 🏢

Salary: Up to $95K




🚀 What we’ll achieve together

You’ll join a strategic technology team driving process optimization, automation, and GenAI initiatives through enterprise-level reporting.

Your work will directly support decision-making by turning complex data into clear, actionable insights.




🎯 Your mission

As a Power BI / Power Platform Analyst, you will design, evolve, and support reporting solutions used across the organization in a structured, data-driven environment.




📅 Your day-to-day

Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports
Translate business requirements into robust data models and insights
Develop solutions using the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse)
Document data sources, logic, and definitions thoroughly
Collaborate with stakeholders across locations 🌍
Train and support end users to drive adoption
Continuously improve report quality and performance




🧰 Your tech stack

Power BI (Desktop & Service)
DAX & Power Query
Power Platform
SQL
BI & data modeling best practices




🧠 You’re a great fit if you:

Have 5+ years of experience, including 3+ years in BI / Analytics
Are a senior hands-on individual contributor
Are comfortable in large, regulated enterprise environments
Communicate clearly and tell compelling stories with data
Enjoy collaboration and documentation




⭐ Nice to have

Experience in finance, investment banking, or financial services
Strong interest in GenAI and automation initiatives
Experience with Snowflake (Cortex is a strong plus)




🎁 Why join Astek Canada?

Up to 4 weeks of vacation + 5 personal days
Comprehensive group insurance (telemedicine & EAP included)
Public transit reimbursement 🚇
Cell phone plan & IT equipment reimbursement 💻
Annual wellness / sports allowance 🏃 ♂️
Referral bonus program
Regular social events & team activities 🎉




🤝 Let’s meet!

If this opportunity speaks to you, apply directly.

Sara, Talent Acquisition Specialist, will reach out for an initial conversation.

You will then meet Floriane, your future manager, to discuss the role, your background, and your career goals.

Job Offer – Power BI / Power Platform Analyst 📊

Location: Montreal

Contract: Permanent

Work model: Hybrid – 3 days/week in office 🏢

Salary: Up to $95K


🚀 What we’ll achieve together

You’ll join a strategic technology team driving process optimization, automation, and GenAI initiatives through enterprise-level reporting.

Your work will directly support decision-making by turning complex data into clear, actionable insights.


🎯 Your mission

As a Power BI / Power Platform Analyst, you will design, evolve, and support reporting solutions used across the organization in a structured, data-driven environment.


📅 Your day-to-day

  • Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports
  • Translate business requirements into robust data models and insights
  • Develop solutions using the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse)
  • Document data sources, logic, and definitions thoroughly
  • Collaborate with stakeholders across locations 🌍
  • Train and support end users to drive adoption
  • Continuously improve report quality and performance


🧰 Your tech stack

  • Power BI (Desktop & Service)
  • DAX & Power Query
  • Power Platform
  • SQL
  • BI & data modeling best practices


🧠 You’re a great fit if you:

  • Have 5+ years of experience, including 3+ years in BI / Analytics
  • Are a senior hands-on individual contributor
  • Are comfortable in large, regulated enterprise environments
  • Communicate clearly and tell compelling stories with data
  • Enjoy collaboration and documentation


⭐ Nice to have

  • Experience in finance, investment banking, or financial services
  • Strong interest in GenAI and automation initiatives
  • Experience with Snowflake (Cortex is a strong plus)


🎁 Why join Astek Canada?

  • Up to 4 weeks of vacation + 5 personal days
  • Comprehensive group insurance (telemedicine & EAP included)
  • Public transit reimbursement 🚇
  • Cell phone plan & IT equipment reimbursement 💻
  • Annual wellness / sports allowance 🏃 ♂️
  • Referral bonus program
  • Regular social events & team activities 🎉

Sara, Talent Acquisition Specialist, will reach out for an initial conversation.

You will then meet Floriane, your future manager, to discuss the role, your background, and your career goals.

Title: Data Analyst, Credit Risk Management Department
Company: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada)
Description:
Department / Branch: Credit Risk Management Department

Reports To: Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department

Employment Type: Full-Time Contract/ Permanent

Location: Richmond Hill Office







Responsibilities

1. Model Development and Optimization

Design, develop, and optimize credit rating models to solve business problems.
Experiment with algorithms, architectures, and frameworks to improve model performance.
Conduct regular validation and tuning of models for accuracy and efficiency.

2. Data Analysis

Work with large datasets, ensuring quality, consistency, and relevance for training models.
Identify patterns and insights to refine model inputs and features.

3. Model Evaluation and Testing

Implement evaluation metrics and validation techniques to ensure model reliability.
Perform testing to compare different models and configurations.

4. Monitoring and Reporting

Deploy models to production environments and monitor their real-time performance.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues to maintain model robustness over time.
Conduct model performance monitoring and reporting.

5. Collaboration and Communication

Collaborate with data stakeholders to align model objectives with business goals.
Communicate findings, methodologies, and results to technical and non-technical audiences .or regulator.

6. Other works assigned by Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department.




Qualifications:

Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Machine Learning, Financial Engineering or related fields.
At least 3 years of experience in financial modelling, preferably within banking or financial services sector.
Strong knowledge in model development, validation, evaluation and optimization.
Proficient in Python, R or similar programming languages.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and teamwork abilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English with work proficiency.
Fluency in Chinese is an asset.

Title: Statistical Data Analyst
Company: York Region (The Regional Municipality of York)
Description:
ABOUT US





Almost 1.2 million residents call York Region home, making it one of the largest regions in Canada – and the fastest growing with a population that’s expected to grow to more than 2 million by 2041. Our geography, which is comprised of about 1,800 square kilometers over nine different municipalities, is as beautiful, interesting and diverse as our people. Local government is organized in a two-tier structure and we work together with our local municipalities to provide residents and businesses access to a broad selection of services and resources.





WHAT WE OFFER





Consistently named one of Canada’s Best Employers by Forbes, we offer a collaborative, progressive workplace that takes pride in our organizational culture and is committed to living The 13+ Factors of Psychological Health and Safety in the Workplace – aligned with our vision to create strong, caring and safe communities both within and outside our walls.






Defined Benefit Pension Program - With the Ontario Municipality Employees Retirement System (OMERS) defined benefit pension plan, you can confidently retire knowing that you will have income for life. Eligibility from date of hire as a full-time employee and includes employer-matched contributions.


Employer of Choice - Recognized as the highest-ranking government employer in Canada and fourth overall among 300 organizations.


Benefits and Wellness - Employees and their loved ones have access to an employee health care spending account, access to a 24/7 Employee and Family Assistance Program and corporate discounts and purchase plans for day-to-day products and services. Casual employees are eligible to receive payment in lieu of benefits and/or vacation.


Inclusive and Diverse Workforce - We’re committed to fostering an environment that celebrates all dimensions of diversity and ensures everyone can develop to their full potential, participate freely in society and live with respect, dignity and freedom from discrimination. Our robust Inclusion, Diversity, Equity and Accessibility program continues to grow, and has been recognized by the United Nations and many other organizations for our leadership.






 



ABOUT THE ROLE

This position is responsible for the extraction, compilation, analysis, reporting and application of statistical data and metrics for programs and services; ensuring data integrity and databases meet data governance requirements. This role is also interpreting, summarizing, creating and maintaining statistical data files; writing reports and dashboards on trends and analysis; designing and developing complex spreadsheet and database applications; contributing to the development and monitoring of surveillance indicators and systems, and developing service profiles.



 





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING






Compiles, analyzes, models and reports on various statistical data from a variety of sources/databases to identify trends that will impact on strategic planning for programs and services and recommends appropriate service delivery strategies to address and/or mitigate the impacts.


Develops and maintains metrics, dashboards, visualizations and reports.


Monitors and reviews database information and conducts data audits, reviews and edits contents to ensure data integrity.


Coordinates and prepares comprehensive data analysis and statistical reports for research, policy, program and service activities and to improve operational efficiency and effectiveness. 


Designs and/or revises complex database and spreadsheet applications including the collection, manipulation and analysis of statistical data.


Prepares reports relating to budget and statistical data to meet Government, Department/Branch/Division objectives and mandates.


Coordinates the collection, reconciliation, submission and reporting of data from within the Department, other Departments and external organizations.


Develops service profiles, utilizing data from various databases and external sources; conducts benchmarking of other program/services to determine and develop best practices for reporting, and process improvements.  


Responds to inquiries and resolves concerns, as appropriate.


Performs other duties as assigned, in accordance with Branch and Department objectives.






 





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR






Successful completion of a University Degree in Business Administration, Mathematics, Statistics, Health Sciences or relevant field, or approved equivalent combination of education and experience


Minimum three (3) years experience in statistical data analysis and data management, including database design, spreadsheet applications and report writing.


Strong report writing, research, analytical and presentation skills and work to tight deadlines.


Demonstrated analytical skills to interpret and reconcile complex statistical reports. 


Demonstrated skills establishing, maintaining and producing complex databases/spreadsheets including current experience with database design, report generation and modifications.

Title: Data Analyst, Product analytics
Description:
Summary

If you’re an experienced data analyst who is ready to take on the challenge of collaborating with stakeholders, delivering insights through data-driven storytelling, and driving analytics initiatives, this could be the right job for you.

Thomson Reuters is seeking a Data Analyst for the Legal Research Product Analytics team. The analyst works as an extension of the product team and collaborates closely with design teams and Senior Analysts to support upcoming decisions and long-term goals.

This role is part of the Thomson Reuters Product Analytics team, a group of approximately 20 analysts of varied backgrounds who share ideas, best practices, and learnings with each other. This is an ideal role for a product or business analyst who is great with data, has some experience with stakeholders and analytics initiatives, and is ready to take the next step in their career.

About The Role

In this opportunity as a Data Analyst - Product Analytics, you will:

Implementation: Partner with senior analysts to establish measurement plans and then work with developers/platform architects to deploy relevant product analytics solutions (like Pendo) and ensure the right metrics, journeys, and events are captured in-product

Center of Excellence: Partner with product teams to define and maintain support processes

Improve Product: Use product analytics/data to recognize patterns and trends to understand user behavior and identify improvement opportunities

BI/Reporting: Build dashboards and reports, including baselines and trends for page performance, feature usage, and customer segments

Thought Leadership: Demonstrate thought leadership by leveraging analyses to understand products, derive insights, run experiments, and deliver business and customer outcomes

About You

You're a fit for the role of Data Analyst - Product Analytics if your background includes:


2-4 years of hands-on product analytics experience with exposure to agile/scrum processes, product backlog grooming, etc.
2+ years of experience using SQL; building and documenting queries for consistent team use.
2+ years of experience using Alteryx or other ETL tools.
2+ years of building data visualizations with products like Tableau, MS Power BI, or similar.
2+ years of experience driving data-driven decision-making within product teams, going beyond report generation to deliver insights. Experience with analyses such as customer journey mapping, audience profiling/segmentation, cohort analysis, tag management, and A/B testing.
Demonstrated experience using statistical packages within Python/R to analyze structured and unstructured datasets and deliver insights on product usage and performance.
Exposure to product analytics tools such as Pendo, Amplitude, Adobe Analytics, and Google Analytics is an asset.
Building and maintaining support processes. Jira, MS Azure DevOps, and MS SharePoint experience is an asset.
Strong communication skills, including experience in supporting and communicating data-driven analyses to various departments and stakeholders.
Legal Research subscription products experience is an asset


Replacement: This position is open due to an existing vacancy to support our evolving business needs.

What’s in it For You?


Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.


Our use of AI within the recruitment process Thomson Reuters utilizes Artificial Intelligence (AI) to support parts of our global recruitment process. Unless you opt-out, our AI system will assess the information provided by you and compare it to the requirements listed for the role, and present the result to our recruitment personnel for further review. The AI system acts as a supporting tool, but there is always a human making the decision if you will be considered for the role

Thomson Reuters complies with local laws that require upfront disclosure of the expected pay range for a position. The base compensation range varies across locations. For Ontario, Canada, the base compensation range for this role is $70,000 CAD - $120,000 CAD. Base pay is positioned within the range based on several factors including an individual’s knowledge, skills and experience with consideration given to internal equity. Base pay is one part of a comprehensive Total Reward program which also includes flexible and supportive benefits and other wellbeing programs. This role may also be eligible for an Annual Bonus based on a combination of enterprise and individual performance.

About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.

Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here.

More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com

Title: Senior Analyst, Analytics & Insights
Description:
The Klick Group—comprising Klick Health (including Klick Katalyst and btwelve), Klick Media Group, Klick Applied Sciences (including Klick Labs), Klick Consulting, and Klick Ventures—is an ecosystem dedicated to realizing the full potential of our people and clients in life sciences. Founded in 1997, we have offices in New York, Philadelphia, Saratoga Springs, Toronto, London, São Paulo, and Singapore. Consistently named a Best Managed Company, Most Admired Corporate Culture, and Great Place to Work, Klick is committed to fostering a high-performance, people-first culture with creativity, collaboration, innovation, and impact across everything we do.

About Our Analytics Craft

Our Analytics craft helps clients use data to understand audiences, optimize engagement, and support meaningful business decisions. The team works across digital measurement, performance analysis, optimization, and predictive modelling, partnering closely with media, strategy, technology, and client services to deliver insights grounded in rigorous analysis and clear communication.

Job Description

This role contributes hands-on analysis and clear storytelling that help senior stakeholders understand performance and guide business decisions. The Senior Analyst translates complex datasets into actionable insights, partners with technical teams to ensure data quality, and supports measurement across digital channels. The role blends analytical rigor, curiosity, and collaboration to support clients across a variety of data-driven needs.

What You’ll Do:


Perform hands-on analysis and deliver insights for senior-level audiences
Define data requirements and collaborate with database specialists to support business questions
Support data collection and measurement across digital channels
Communicate findings clearly to internal and client stakeholders
Apply quantitative techniques to help improve performance and decision-making


Qualifications

Required:


5+ years of hands-on data analysis experience with large, multi-dimensional datasets
3+ years working with web analytics platforms, including Google Analytics
Familiarity with tag management systems such as Google Tag Manager, Tealium, or Ensighten
Experience analyzing digital marketing channels such as media, CRM, or mobile apps
Knowledge of statistical analysis principles and methods
Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and analysis


Desired:


Experience working with WebTrends or SiteCatalyst
Ability to connect analytical findings to business outcomes in client-facing settings
Experience with predictive modelling or marketing optimization approaches
Strong communication skills for explaining analytical concepts to non-technical audiences
Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data and measurement needs
Curiosity for new analytical methods, tools, and emerging digital-measurement approaches


Additional Information

This role is backfilling an existing vacancy at Klick. The base salary range for this position is between CAD $85,000 - $105,000. The final amount offered will be determined based on several factors such as a candidate's work location, their unique skill set, education, and prior work experience. Founded in 1997, we have offices in New York, Philadelphia, Saratoga Springs, Toronto, London, São Paulo, and Singapore. Consistently named a Best Managed Company, Most Admired Corporate Culture, and Great Place to Work, Klick is committed to fostering a high-performance, people-first culture with creativity, collaboration, innovation, and impact across everything we do.

About Our Analytics Craft

Our Analytics craft helps clients use data to understand audiences, optimize engagement, and support meaningful business decisions. The team works across digital measurement, performance analysis, optimization, and predictive modelling, partnering closely with media, strategy, technology, and client services to deliver insights grounded in rigorous analysis and clear communication.

Job Description

This role contributes hands-on analysis and clear storytelling that help senior stakeholders understand performance and guide business decisions. The Senior Analyst translates complex datasets into actionable insights, partners with technical teams to ensure data quality, and supports measurement across digital channels. The role blends analytical rigor, curiosity, and collaboration to support clients across a variety of data-driven needs.

What You’ll Do:

  • Perform hands-on analysis and deliver insights for senior-level audiences
  • Define data requirements and collaborate with database specialists to support business questions
  • Support data collection and measurement across digital channels
  • Communicate findings clearly to internal and client stakeholders
  • Apply quantitative techniques to help improve performance and decision-making

Qualifications

Required:

  • 5+ years of hands-on data analysis experience with large, multi-dimensional datasets
  • 3+ years working with web analytics platforms, including Google Analytics
  • Familiarity with tag management systems such as Google Tag Manager, Tealium, or Ensighten
  • Experience analyzing digital marketing channels such as media, CRM, or mobile apps
  • Knowledge of statistical analysis principles and methods
  • Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and analysis

Desired:

  • Experience working with WebTrends or SiteCatalyst
  • Ability to connect analytical findings to business outcomes in client-facing settings
  • Experience with predictive modelling or marketing optimization approaches
  • Strong communication skills for explaining analytical concepts to non-technical audiences
  • Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data and measurement needs
  • Curiosity for new analytical methods, tools, and emerging digital-measurement approaches

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
StackAdapt is the leading technology company that empowers marketers to reach, engage, and convert audiences with precision. With 465 billion automated optimizations per second, the AI-powered StackAdapt Marketing Platform seamlessly connects brand and performance marketing to drive measurable results across the entire customer journey. The most forward-thinking marketers choose StackAdapt to orchestrate high-impact campaigns across programmatic advertising and marketing channels.

Who are we:

StackAdapt is a self-serve advertising platform that specializes in multi-channel solutions including native, display, video, connected TV, audio, in-game, and digital out-of-home ads. We empower hundreds of digitally-focused companies to deliver outcomes and exceptional campaign performance everyday. StackAdapt was founded with a vision to be more than an advertising platform, it’s a hub of innovation, imagination and creativity.

The Business Data Analyst role is a unique combination of technical execution and internal customer relationship management. You will serve as a "context-shifter"—equally comfortable navigating complex SQL databases as you are advocating for the needs of our business partners. We are looking for a versatile professional who doesn't just pull data, but seeks to understand the "why" behind every metric to ensure our business objectives are met with precision.

StackAdapt is a remote first company. We are open to hire residents of Canada.


What You'll Be Doing:
Stakeholder Partnership: Act as a customer-facing advocate for the business. You will lead discovery sessions to understand requirements, define key metrics, and draft Business Requirement Documents (BRDs).
Partner with RevOps, Marketing Ops, and CRM administrators to maintain data integrity across systems (e.g., Salesforce), including field-level metric population, account structure governance, and cross-system reconciliation.
CRM & Cross-functional Data Stewardship — Partner with RevOps, Marketing Ops, and CRM administrators to maintain data integrity across systems (e.g., Salesforce), including field-level metric population, account structure governance, and cross-system reconciliation.
Technical Execution: Execute project work and "hands-on-keyboard" development. This includes writing complex SQL queries, navigating disparate databases, and investigating data sets to find "the truth."
Requirement Translation: Bridge the gap between technical and non-technical teams by translating high-level business goals into actionable technical specifications.
Data Discovery & Troubleshooting: Serve as the first line of defense for troubleshooting data issues and fulfilling ad-hoc data requests to support immediate business needs.
Pipeline Foundation: In your first 3–6 months, you will focus heavily on building and optimizing data pipes to ensure a robust foundation for future analysis.
What You'll Bring to the Table
Relational Database Proficiency — Deep comfort navigating unfamiliar RDBMS environments, including complex joins, schema exploration, data lineage investigation, and query optimization. Experience with cloud data warehouses such as Snowflake is a plus.
Exceptional Communication: Strong soft skills with a track record of translating technical concepts for executive-level stakeholders.
Versatility: The ability to "live in multiple contexts" and pivot quickly from deep technical work to strategic business meetings.
Technical Curiosity: You are proactive about personal projects and stay up-to-date with industry trends, including a high proficiency in using LLMs to accelerate workflows.
ETL/ELT Knowledge: Hands-on experience building and maintaining data pipelines.
LLM/AI Literacy — Beyond using LLMs to accelerate personal workflows, an appreciation for how data model design (e.g., star schemas, clear naming conventions) improves AI/ML system integration and reliability.
Python (Plus): Experience with Python for automation or advanced analysis is a nice-to-have.
 

The compensation range listed for this role reflects the expected base salary for candidates located in Ontario, Canada. It is informed by market data and the approved budget for this position. StackAdapt maintains different compensation ranges for roles across other countries and regions, and final offers will be aligned to the candidate’s current location.This range represents base salary only. Depending on the role, candidates may also be eligible for additional compensation such as annual bonuses, commissions, equity awards, and a comprehensive benefits package.Factors Influencing Final Compensation:The final compensation offer will be determined by a variety of factors, which may include, but are not limited to: the candidate's specific experience, technical skills, knowledge, abilities, and relevant education, licensure, and certifications.Other business factors, such as organizational needs and budget alignment, may also be considered in the final offer.

Ontario Salary Band: $84,000 CAD - $99,000 CAD

StackAdapter's Enjoy:
Highly competitive salary
Retirement/ 401K/ Pension Savings globally
Competitive Paid time off packages including birthday's off!
Access to a comprehensive mental health care program
Health benefits from day one of employment
Work from home reimbursements
Optional global WeWork membership for those who want a change from their home office and hubs in London and Toronto
Robust training and onboarding program
Coverage and support of personal development initiatives (conferences, courses, books etc)
Access to StackAdapt programmatic courses and certifications to support continuous learning
An awesome parental leave program
A friendly, welcoming, and supportive culture
Our social and team events!

Please note: Benefits and perks may vary depending on your country of employment and the nature of your engagement. In locations where StackAdapt does not have a legal entity, employment and benefits are administered in accordance with local regulations and partner policies.

StackAdapt is a diverse and inclusive team of collaborative, hardworking individuals trying to make a dent in the universe. No matter who you are, where you are from, who you love, follow in faith, disability (or superpower) status, ethnicity, or the gender you identify with (if you’re comfortable, let us know your pronouns), you are welcome at StackAdapt. If you have any requests or requirements to support you throughout any part of the interview process, please let our Talent team know. We use artificial intelligence (AI) to streamline the resume reviews of candidates and assess their fit based on the criteria outlined in the job posting. We do not use AI to make any final hiring or interview decisions. About StackAdapt We've been recognized for our diverse and supportive workplace, high performing campaigns, award-winning customer service, and innovation. We've been awarded: G2 Top Software for 2026
2026 Best Workplaces™ for Young Talent and in Canada by Great Place to Work®
#1 DSP on G2 and leader in a number of categories including Cross-Channel Advertising To learn more about our privacy practices, please see our Privacy Policy.

Title: BUSINESS ANALYST 311 (Data Analyst)
Description:
Job ID: 62402
Job Category: Policy, Planning & Research
Division & Section: Customer Experience, Business Intelligence & Workforce
Work Location: METRO HALL
Job Type & Duration: Full-time, 1 Permanent Vacancy
Salary Range: $89,337.00 - $120,831.00
Hiring Zone: $93,692.00 - $101,582.00
Shift Information: Monday to Friday, 35 hours per week
Affiliation: Non-Union
Number of Positions Open: 1
Posting Period: 23-APR-2026 to 04-MAY-2026




Job Summary:

To conduct research and analysis for 311 Toronto, identify a range of feasible options for operations, policies, technology, information systems and management practices and to assess the implications and viability of each option and recommending changes to improve service delivery.




Major Responsibilities:

Implements detailed plans and recommends policies/procedures regarding program specific requirements.
Conducts research into assigned area ensuring that such research takes into account developments within the field, corporate policies and practices, legislation and initiatives by other levels of government.
Provides input into and administers assigned budget, ensuring that expenditures are controlled and maintained within approved budget limitations.
Provides leadership on the implementation of 311 initiatives.
Develops options for policies, procedures, business process reviews, management practices, and recommends changes to support management functions and improve efficiency and effectiveness of service delivery.
Manages one or more subprojects of the 311 Initiative.
Co-ordinates the implementation of assigned subprojects with other components of the 311 initiative and/or other e-City or other initiatives.
Develops and documents, with key stakeholders, standard operating policies and procedures and develops appropriate training programs.
Supports the project management office and leads inter-divisional teams and working groups.
Assists in building and managing co-operative relationships with other City divisions to ensure adequate resources are available to achieve the project goals.
Prepares visual presentations, briefing materials and other documentation as required.
Provides presentation support, and gives presentations as required.
Develops terms of reference and methodologies for review.
Consults and provides strategic advice to 311 management regarding research, reports and corporate initiatives.
Monitors current social, economic and political issues that impact relevant policy directions for 311.
Reports on status of reports to 311 management.
Deals with confidential and sensitive information affecting 311 and its resources.
Provides support and recommendations related to confidential business realignments with 311.
Manages vendors and external contractors.
Prepares Request for Information/Purchase/Quotations (RFI/P/Q) and other formal project documentation.
Takes an active role as a member of the project team and contributes to the overall work planning and priority setting of the project.

Key Qualifications:

Post‑secondary education in a relevant discipline (e.g., Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Business Intelligence, Business Analysis), or an equivalent combination of education and related experience.
Extensive practical experience with Power BI, along with strong proficiency in relational data modeling, SQL and DAX development, Power Query transformations, and building performant, scalable BI solutions. Possessing a Microsoft Power BI certification (PL-300 or equivalent) is desirable.
Proven experience collecting, cleaning, transforming, analyzing, and interpreting large and complex datasets, including qualitative, quantitative analysis, and spatial analysis.
Demonstrated ability to design and deliver intuitive, stakeholder‑specific dashboards and analytical reports using Power BI and other industry‑standard BI tools.
Excellent data storytelling and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex analysis and insights to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Demonstrated political acuity, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to work effectively independently, as well as collaboratively in a fast‑paced, evolving environment.
Excellent time‑management, organizational skills, and a demonstrated ability to maintain confidentiality and discretion.
Knowledge of producing accessible reports compliant with AODA requirements.
Considerable experience conducting business process reviews and analysis, identifying best practices and service improvement opportunities, and developing evidence-based recommendations to support business transformation initiatives.
Demonstrated technical aptitude and logical problem-solving skills, with experience working in or supporting enterprise systems (e.g., Salesforce) and the ability to translate business requirements into system configuration and process solutions.
Experience supporting or coordinating transformational change initiatives impacting processes, people, and technology.
Considerable experience using technologies such as Microsoft Office products including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio and SharePoint. Experience using Jira and Confluence is desirable.
Experience with Salesforce reports and dashboards, Verint, Cisco/Genesys is an asset.
Experience supporting call centers, performance reporting is an asset




Equity, Diversity and Inclusion

The City is an equal opportunity employer, dedicated to creating a workplace culture of inclusiveness that reflects the diverse residents that we serve. Learn more about the City’s commitment to employment equity.




Accommodation

The City of Toronto is committed to creating an accessible and inclusive organization. We are committed to providing barrier-free and accessible employment practices in compliance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA). Should you require Code-protected accommodation through any stage of the recruitment process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to meet your needs. Disability-related accommodation during the application process is available upon request. Learn more about the City’s Hiring Policies and Accommodation Process.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
ID Logistics is a leading international contract logistics company with operations in more than 19 countries worldwide providing innovative supply chain solutions for some of the world’s largest retailers and manufacturers. As the first site in Canada, our focus is on operational excellence, safety, and customer satisfaction, with a strong commitment to teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement. 




ID Logistics Canada – XYY1 in Ajax, ON is looking for an ICQA Data Analyst to join our growing team for three (3) months. Reporting to the Stock Manager the ICQA Data Analyst will be responsible for the following:




Responsibilities:




· Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets related to inventory accuracy, cycle counts, quality trends, and operational performance.
 · Develop daily, weekly, and monthly ICQA reports, dashboards, and KPIs for leadership review.
 · Identify discrepancies, patterns, and root causes impacting inventory accuracy and quality assurance.
 · Support cycle count processes by validating results and tracking variances.
 · Conduct deep-dive investigations into inventory issues, shrink, and process gaps.
 · Partner with Operations Supervisors, Inventory Control, and Quality teams to implement corrective actions.
 · Monitor key ICQA performance metrics such as accuracy %, defect rate, count compliance, and audit results.
 · Maintain data integrity through validation, reconciliation, and error-checking processes.
 · Present insights and findings to management to support data-driven decision making.
 · Support ongoing and ad-hoc ICQA projects during the contract term.




The above responsibilities describe the general nature and level of work being performed. They are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibility, duties and skills required for this position. Additional duties may be assigned as needed.




Qualifications:




Post-secondary education in Data Analytics, Supply Chain, Business, Mathematics, or related field.
 Proven experience in data analysis, preferably in a warehouse, logistics, 3PL, or fulfillment environment.
 Strong proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, formulas, Power Query an asset).
 Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
 Knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) or inventory software is an asset.
 Understanding of ICQA principles, cycle counts, root-cause analysis, and inventory workflows.
 Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving abilities.
 Ability to communicate complex data insights clearly and concisely.
 Ability to work independently and meet deadlines within a fast-paced environment.




Accessibility & Transparency at ID Logistics Canada
 At ID Logistics Canada, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace. We welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and will provide accommodations throughout the recruitment, selection, and employment process upon request. If you require accommodation at any stage, please contact us and we will work with you to meet your needs.




In alignment with Ontario’s Pay Transparency and Employment Standards, ID Logistics Canada provides clear information on compensation and working conditions. We do not require Canadian work experience as a condition of employment and are dedicated to fair, transparent, and respectful hiring practices. Applicants will be informed of the outcome of their application within the required timelines, and all postings and related materials are retained in accordance with provincial legislation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Specialist

Our client is looking for a Data Specialist to join their Technology and Innovation department. As the Data Specialist, you will design and document workflow, integrate and customize applications, and make appropriate system controls and protocol recommendations that will positively impact the operational effectiveness of all aspects concerning data assets and offerings. This position is a functional expert on specified applications and will be the main point of contact between the Association and Vendor support. This role is based on-site in Edmonton.

Your Success Will Be Defined By Your Ability To


Extract data from MLS System, Dynamics CRM, and external sources. Maintain databases
Use appropriate software to organize and analyze large volumes of data and continuously improve and automate data solutions and processes
Conduct full-cycle data analytics activities and ETL processes
Format data that meets specific rules/conditions and combine it with formulas to help end users highlight data (i.e. statistics)
Detect and correct data load exceptions or inaccuracies
Combine Excel with SQL, and automatically pull data from a database (ex. querying data from SQL into Excel)
Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements, design, and deliver reports and provide data management improvements
Utilize data management tools to monitor and control members' access to RETS and/or Web API, ensuring authorization is granted and restricted as needed while preventing data transaction volumes from exceeding the threshold
Assist Members, vendors, and other stakeholders with requests to access the Association's MLS Systems Data. Review applications that use the data
Investigate and resolve issues related to RETS and Web API, for Members, vendors, and stakeholders
File, store, and maintain all contracts between members/vendors/stakeholders
Notify members, vendors, and stakeholders of issues with data in the MLS Systems
Communicate with non-compliant users regarding data on their websites or software
Collaborate with the Director of Technology to create and execute project work plans
Execute assigned technology projects in relation to data and statistics.
Attend all staff and department-specific meetings as well as training sessions
Research new methods to optimize data analytics and daily business operations


Your strengths include:


Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in Computer Engineering, Information Technology, or related field is required
Proven experience (1-5 years) working in an IT role involving technology development and deployment, or the equivalent, is required
Previous experience using Microsoft PowerBI and/or the equivalent is essential
Familiarity with Paragon or other MLS tools is advantageous, as is knowledge of Microsoft Dynamics CRM or similar program


If you are interested in this role and meet the above criteria, please click the “Apply” button to send your resume directly to Lipali Patnaik.

Title: Analyst
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an Analyst in Canada.

This role sits within a dynamic finance and accounting environment, focused on supporting the delivery of accurate, timely, and high-quality financial and regulatory reporting. You will contribute to the preparation, validation, and analysis of financial data used for internal decision-making, external reporting, and regulatory compliance. The position requires strong analytical capability and attention to detail, with an emphasis on ensuring data integrity and effective financial controls. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather, structure, and interpret financial information that supports both operational and strategic objectives. Operating in a fast-paced and regulated environment, you will play a key role in identifying trends, resolving data issues, and improving reporting processes. This is a strong opportunity for a detail-oriented finance professional to grow within a structured and high-impact organization.

Accountabilities

This role is responsible for supporting financial reporting processes, ensuring data accuracy, and contributing to regulatory and management reporting activities across finance and accounting functions.


Support the preparation and execution of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial and regulatory reporting processes.
Collect, validate, and organize financial data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency across reports and dashboards.
Analyze financial information to identify trends, variances, risks, and opportunities for improvement.
Assist in the interpretation and application of internal policies, accounting standards, and regulatory requirements.
Contribute to audit processes by providing documentation and supporting internal and external audit requests.
Develop reports, summaries, and data visualizations to support business and management decision-making.
Ensure strong governance and control practices are followed across financial data and reporting activities.
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to support continuous improvement of reporting processes and tools.


Requirements

This role requires strong analytical skills, financial reporting knowledge, and the ability to work independently in a structured and compliance-driven environment.


3-5 years of relevant experience in finance, accounting, reporting, or a related analytical role, or equivalent education and experience.
Strong understanding of financial reporting processes and data management principles.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to present insights clearly.
Ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong collaboration and teamwork skills across cross-functional stakeholders.
Proficiency in analyzing and interpreting financial data to support decision-making.
Knowledge of regulatory and compliance environments is considered an asset.


Benefits


Competitive salary ranging from $50,100 to $93,000 CAD annually
Performance-based incentives and discretionary bonus opportunities
Comprehensive health, dental, life, and accident insurance coverage
Retirement savings and pension plan options
Tuition reimbursement and professional development support
Inclusive and collaborative work environment focused on growth
Exposure to large-scale financial reporting and regulatory processes
Strong internal learning, mentoring, and career development opportunities


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Associate Data Engineer
Description:
Requisition ID: 261668

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Purpose

Contributes to the overall success of the Data Engineering Team under GOCT Solution Architecture & Engineering. Ensures specific individual goals, plans, initiatives are executed / delivered in support of the business strategies and objectives to comply with the governing regulations, internal policies, and procedures

Accountability


Support and maintain enterprise server infrastructure within Microsoft environments, ensuring high availability, system stability, and alignment with enterprise standards.
Manage end-to-end software lifecycle activities including vulnerability remediation, patching, and software currency upgrades to keep servers secure and compliant.
Lead and support infrastructure upgrade and migration initiatives, including SQL Server version upgrades to Microsoft-supported platforms, ensuring minimal business disruption.
Conduct server and infrastructure security assessments to identify risks, evaluate exposure, and collaborate with security teams to implement remediation and control measures.
Configure and maintain enterprise connectivity components such as ODBC drivers, data adapters, and integration tools to ensure reliable data access across systems.
Provide production support for enterprise data pipelines and ETL processes, including proactive monitoring of daily jobs to ensure successful and timely execution.
Troubleshoot and resolve production incidents, ETL failures, and data issues by working closely with development and support teams to restore service quickly and prevent recurrence.
Manage deployment activities and change requests, supporting application and infrastructure migrations across Development, UAT, and Production environments with proper controls.
Support cloud and hybrid data platforms including Azure Data Factory pipelines and Power BI Report Server, handling deployments and troubleshooting.
Ensure compliance with data governance and security policies by managing SQL Server access controls, conducting PII and risk assessments, and supporting application portfolio management and ServiceNow processes.
Understands how the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions.
Actively pursues effective and efficient operations of his/her respective areas in accordance with Scotiabank’s Values, its Code of Conduct, and the Global Sales Principles, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational, compliance, AML/ATF/sanctions and conduct risk.


Experience


5+ years of experience supporting and maintaining complex data and server infrastructure to enable data analytics and business intelligence reporting
Strong technical expertise in SQL Server, SSIS, and T-SQL with hands-on experience in ETL development and troubleshooting.
Experience supporting enterprise data infrastructure and servers, including production support, issue resolution, and maintaining system security and stability.
Proven experience with ServiceNow incident management system.
Experience with reporting tools such as Power BI Report Server, Power BI Services, and Power BI Desktop.
Exposure to Azure Data Factory pipeline development and deployments is an added advantage.
Knowledge of Canadian banking systems, particularly Retail and International Business Banking, is considered an asset.
Good understanding of software development lifecycle, testing, and evaluation practices.
Familiarity with data warehousing concepts and methodologies.
Strong verbal and written communication skills.


Education

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field (or equivalent experience).

Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data & Reporting Analyst
Description:
💼 Are you someone who enjoys transforming raw data into meaningful business insights while helping teams make smarter, data-driven decisions?




We're hiring a Data & Reporting Analyst to join a well-established, collaborative organization where data plays a critical role in supporting business operations, reporting, and decision-making across multiple departments.




This is an opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and enjoys working across reporting, business intelligence, data warehousing, and analytics. You'll work closely with business users to understand reporting needs, build scalable reporting solutions, and help improve how data is leveraged across the organization.




💻 Who You Are




A detail-oriented data professional who enjoys solving business problems through data and analytics
Someone who can confidently work with both technical teams and business stakeholders
Experienced in building reports, dashboards, and BI solutions within Microsoft-based environments
A strong analytical thinker with a passion for data accuracy and continuous improvement
Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Someone who enjoys both independent work and cross-functional collaboration
A curious and adaptable individual who enjoys learning new technologies and tools




📍 Work Type




Location: North York, ON (Fully Onsite - 5 days a week in office)
Vacancy Type: This role reflects an existing vacancy




🎯 What You'll Do




Meet with business users to gather reporting and data requirements
Build reports and dashboards using SSRS, SQL Server, and Excel
Develop and maintain cubes and dimensional data models using SSAS and MDX
Support and enhance SQL-based data warehouse environments
Assist end users with data analysis, interpretation, and reporting needs
Troubleshoot reporting, data, and integration issues as they arise
Support testing activities including unit testing, integration testing, and user acceptance testing
Create and maintain documentation related to reporting and data processes
Research and resolve technical issues using available resources and tools
Contribute to ongoing improvements across reporting, analytics, and BI processes
Stay current with evolving technologies, tools, and best practices within data and analytics




⭐ What We're Looking For




4+ years of experience in data analysis, reporting, business intelligence, or related roles
Strong experience working within Microsoft SQL Server environments (SQL 2017+)
Hands-on experience with SSRS, SSAS, and SSIS
Advanced T-SQL experience including DDL and DML queries
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and BI solution development
Experience building dashboards and reports for business users
Knowledge of dimensional modeling and relational database design
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills including pivot tables, formulas, and VBA
Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving capabilities
Excellent communication skills with the ability to work directly with end users and stakeholders
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment




Nice to have:




Experience with Power BI or Jet Reports
Professional certifications related to Microsoft, BI, or Data Analytics are considered an asset




🔥 What Makes This Role Exciting




Impact: Your work will directly support business decision-making across multiple departments
Variety: Balance reporting, analytics, troubleshooting, and business collaboration in one role
Collaboration: Work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders across the organization
Stability: Join a well-established organization with long-term growth and stability




Benefits




Base Salary: $70,000 - $80,000
Paid Time Off: Competitive vacation and personal days
Health & Dental Benefits: Comprehensive coverage to support your overall well-being

Title: Data Annotator
Description:
Company Description

IT Banque is a leading AI consulting and solutions provider focused on integrating artificial intelligence into real-world business applications. Our services range from fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) to custom AI tool development, helping businesses improve automation and operational efficiency. With expertise in dataset curation, model evaluation, generative AI integration, and scalable infrastructure deployment, we deliver practical AI solutions tailored to business needs.

Role Description

This is a remote contract / freelance opportunity for a Data Annotator. The Data Annotator will be responsible for labeling, categorizing, and tagging datasets used to train and improve AI models. Daily responsibilities include reviewing data for quality assurance, maintaining annotation accuracy and consistency, and following project-specific guidelines.

The role may also involve collaborating with AI and data teams to improve annotation workflows and contribute to the development of high-quality datasets for AI applications such as natural language processing, speech recognition, and generative AI systems.

This position offers flexible working hours and is suitable for candidates seeking part-time, freelance, or project-based remote work.

Qualifications
Experience with data annotation, labeling, categorization, or content review tasks
Strong attention to detail with the ability to maintain high accuracy and consistency
Basic understanding of AI, machine learning, or generative AI concepts
Good organizational and time management skills
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Ability to work independently in a remote environment
Experience using annotation platforms or data labeling tools is a plus
Familiarity with spreadsheets, collaborative tools, or project management platforms is an advantage
High school diploma or equivalent required; additional certifications or relevant education is a plus
Multilingual ability is a plus, especially English, Chinese, Japanese, or French

Title: Freelance Data Science Engineer (Python & SQL)
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $55 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Remote role in Toronto, ON. Working hours are 8:30 AM - 5 PM EST. Must come in to the office at least once a Week.




We’re looking for a Senior Data Analyst with strong experience in BI, big data, and stakeholder-facing analytics to join a high-impact team.




Must-have skills:

Power BI & Looker (dashboarding, reporting)
PySpark & Snowflake SQL (large-scale data processing)
Strong communication (requirement gathering, stakeholder alignment)




Job Description:

At least 5 years of experience in a data analysis role
5+ years of experience in Python, PySpark & Snowflake SQL, and visualization tools like Power BI or Looker.
A proven track record of decision making and problem solving based on analytics. Conceptual thinking skills must be complemented by a strong quantitative orientation.
Strong business judgment, leadership, and integrity. You should be a tenacious decision maker, able to bring a healthy, aggressive, yet responsible approach to business.
Excellent written and oral communication skills, coupled with strategic influencing skills and the ability to drive agreement through intellect, interpersonal and negotiation.
Self-starter and adaptable. You’re energized and thrive in a fast-paced environment.
Solid understanding of databases and proficient in writing PySpark or native SQL in systems like Hive, Snowflake and Presto.
Strong skills in BI tools, Looker and Excel.
Drives initiatives independently with minimal supervision.
Airflow, ML techniques, Spark debugging are nice to have




Bonus Points:

Skilled in using data mining techniques, including supervised and unsupervised learning.
Experienced in analyzing and debugging data with Spark/PySpark.
Familiar with Airflow for workflow management.
Able to collaborate effectively within a cross-functional, matrix environment.

Title: Computer Systems Analyst | Remote
Description:
Position: Computer Systems Analyst

Type: Contract

Compensation: $66 - $129/hour

Location: Remote

Commitment: 10-40 hrs/week

Role Responsibilities


Analyze and design system architecture to support evolving business needs, focusing on ERP systems such as GSAP, Oracle, and Dynamics.
Develop and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, ensuring clarity and compliance with data privacy standards.
Collaborate cross-functionally with stakeholders and subject matter experts to gather requirements and drive workflow optimization.
Lead and execute system integration initiatives, ensuring seamless interoperability and robust data flow across platforms.
Conduct rigorous system testing and user acceptance testing (UAT) to validate system performance and user requirements.
Proactively identify and resolve technical challenges, leveraging strong problem-solving skills and industry best practices.


Requirements


Have a minimum of of experience as a Computer Systems Analyst or in a similar role within complex enterprise environments.
Demonstrate the ability to create detailed technical documentation and manage sensitive data with strict adherence to privacy regulations.
Possess expertise in ERP systems (GSAP, Oracle, Dynamics) and hands-on experience with SQL Developer for data analysis and integration.
Have a proven track record in workflow optimization and process improvement initiatives.
Exhibit excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a knack for cross-functional collaboration in remote settings.


Application Process


Easy Apply on LinkedIn
Check email for next steps
Participate in resume evaluation & interview stage
