Job Description:

  • Seeking a Specialist with 7 to 9 years of experience in Apache Spark and Python to design develop and optimize scalable data processing solutions within the Python Data domain
  • Develop and maintain scalable and robust data pipelines using Apache Spark and Python Collaborate with data scientists and analysts to enhance and optimize data workflows
  • Ensure data quality integrity and consistency across large and complex datasets
  • Implement efficient data transformations and aggregations to support analytics and reporting Integrate data solutions seamlessly into existing systems by working closely with cross functional teams
  • Monitor and troubleshoot data processing jobs to maintain high reliability and performance
  • Stay updated with emerging trends technologies and best practices in big data and Python development

Roles and Responsibilities

  • Design develop and optimize high performance data processing applications leveraging Apache Spark
  • Write clean reusable and efficient Python code to support various data engineering initiatives
  • Analyse complex datasets to extract actionable insights and identify trends
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and deliver tailored data solutions that meet business objectives
  • Mentor junior team members and promote knowledge sharing within the team
  • Participate actively in code reviews to ensure adherence to coding standards and best practices
  • Manage deployment of data pipelines and continuously monitor their health and performance in production environments

Title: Data Steward
Description:
Location: Greater Toronto Area, ON, Hybrid

Language: English, written and spoken communication required




About the Opportunity

An established infrastructure and construction organization is seeking a Sr. Data Steward to join its Enterprise Analytics and Data Governance team. This is an opportunity to play a meaningful role in shaping how enterprise data is governed, documented, and leveraged across a large and evolving organization that supports critical infrastructure projects across North America.

In this role, you’ll collaborate closely with Information Services, business leaders, and technical teams to strengthen data quality, governance practices, and enterprise data literacy. You’ll help create a more trusted and discoverable data environment while supporting modernization initiatives that improve how teams access and use information to drive decision-making.




What’s In It for You

Join a collaborative and people-focused environment that values innovation, continuous improvement, and knowledge sharing. You’ll have the opportunity to work alongside cross-functional teams on enterprise-wide initiatives that directly support operational excellence and long-term growth.

This organization is committed to employee development and offers a culture that encourages learning, inclusion, and career progression. You’ll gain exposure to modern governance tools and practices while contributing to impactful, future-focused work.




Your Responsibilities

• You’ll maintain and evolve the enterprise Business Glossary and Data Dictionary, ensuring consistency and clarity across critical business data elements

• In this role, you’ll partner with Information Services and business stakeholders to steward metadata, data lineage, and enterprise data inventories

• You’ll collaborate with data owners and Information Security teams to support data classification and governance standards

• You’ll support data discovery efforts by validating data sources, contextual meaning, and fitness for use across analytical environments

• You’ll document, track, and resolve data quality and governance issues raised by business users and stakeholders

• In this role, you’ll work closely with Data Analysts, Engineers, and SMEs to improve governed data access and strengthen enterprise data literacy




Skills and Qualifications

• 3+ years of experience in Data Governance, Data Management, or Data Analysis roles

• Undergraduate degree in Computer Science, Information Management, Business Technology Management, or a related discipline

• Hands-on experience with metadata management, data cataloging, master data management, and enterprise data quality practices

• Experience with governance and MDM platforms such as IBM Match 360, IBM Watsonx Data Intelligence, Collibra, Alation, or Informatica

• Working knowledge of SQL and data modeling concepts is considered an asset

• Strong communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences

• Exceptional attention to detail, documentation abilities, and experience working cross-functionally in complex environments




Note from the Hiring Manager

“We’re looking for someone who enjoys bringing structure to complex data environments and can build strong relationships across teams. This role is ideal for someone who is curious, collaborative, and passionate about improving how organizations use and trust their data.”




Why Partner with Altis

If you’ve never worked with a staffing agency before, we make it easy. We work with top employers across Canada who have great jobs to fill, each vetted and verified by our team. When you apply for a job with Altis, we get to know you as a candidate and learn what your strengths are. Then, if you’re a solid match, we handle all the logistics, advocating for you as a candidate for the role, providing access to coaching and connecting you directly with the hiring manager. And rest assured, all our services are free of cost for candidates.

Title: Data Engineer (Early Career) (Canada)
Description:
Jobright is your personal AI job search agent transforming the job search process. They are seeking an early-career Data Engineer to design and build data pipelines, ensuring data quality and collaborating with analytics and product teams.




Why Join Us




• Build real, production AI agents used by real users

• High ownership and impact

• Work at the intersection of AI, agents, and product

• Shape how people experience AI-driven job search




Responsibilities




• Design and build reliable data pipelines that move data from diverse sources into centralized warehouses and lakes for downstream consumption

• Own the full lifecycle of ETL/ELT processes, from schema design and ingestion to transformation, testing, and deployment

• Ensure data quality and consistency by implementing validation checks, anomaly detection, and automated alerting across critical pipelines

• Partner with analytics, data science, and product teams to understand their data needs and deliver clean, well-documented datasets

• Optimize query performance, storage costs, and pipeline reliability as data volumes and complexity grow




Qualification




Required




• Recent graduate or early-career professional (0–2 years of experience) with a degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related technical field

• Strong proficiency in Python and SQL, with hands-on experience writing and debugging data transformation logic

• Practical understanding of data modeling, warehouse design patterns, and the trade-offs between batch and streaming architectures

• Solid software engineering foundations, including comfort with APIs, version control (Git), and writing testable, production-ready code




Preferred




• Previous internship or project experience building end-to-end data pipelines or managing datasets at non-trivial scale

• Familiarity with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks

• Hands-on exposure to orchestration tools like Airflow, dbt, or Prefect, and streaming technologies like Kafka or Spark Streaming

• Curiosity about how data powers product decisions, and a willingness to dig into messy, ambiguous data problems

Title: Data & ML Engineer
Description:
About Jetson

Jetson is on a mission to accelerate the transition of 100 million homes across North America away from fossil fuels toward sustainable energy use. We believe in a future that is 100% electric and 100% better.

Homes are one of the largest sources of carbon emissions, yet adoption of solutions like heat pumps remains slow due to cost and complexity. Jetson is solving this by building the first fully vertically integrated home electrification company — making clean energy simple, transparent, and affordable.

We rely heavily on technology, automation, and data to scale this impact. At Jetson, we value people who are excellent at their craft, curious about new tools (including AI), and motivated to continuously improve how work gets done.

The Opportunity


Build ML systems that interact with the physical world. You’ll gain experience applying data and machine learning to real-world control problems—where feedback loops, delayed effects, and physical constraints matter as much as algorithms.
See the full picture, and influence it. At Jetson, you’ll see how data flows from devices in homes, through cloud systems, into models, and back into product behavior. That context will make you a stronger engineer and a better decision-maker.
Shape our data and ML foundations. We’re early, and that’s an advantage. You’ll help define our data architecture, modeling standards, evaluation practices, and how we responsibly use AI to move faster and build better products.


What You'll Do


Ownership of data and ML systems end-to-end. You’ll design, build, and maintain pipelines and models that power core product functionality—energy optimization, scheduling intelligence, anomaly detection, forecasting, and more. This includes data ingestion, feature engineering, training, deployment, monitoring, and iteration.
Strong foundations in data engineering. You’re comfortable working with production data systems: streaming and batch pipelines, data modeling, warehouses/lakes, and observability. You know from experience that reliable ML starts with reliable data.
Applied ML experience. You’ve built and shipped models into production, and you understand the tradeoffs involved - model complexity vs. robustness, retraining cadence, drift detection, and graceful failure modes. Experience with time-series data, optimization, or control systems is a plus.
Systems and API design judgment. You’ll design interfaces between ML systems, backend services, and edge devices. You think carefully about contracts, versioning, and what happens when predictions are wrong or unavailable.
Engineering rigor. You write maintainable, well-tested code. You value code review, automated testing, and CI/CD—not as overhead, but as enablers of speed and reliability in production ML systems.


What You Bring


Comfortable operating without a playbook. You thrive in environments where the data is messy, the problem isn’t fully defined, and the right answer isn’t obvious. You’re able to frame problems, make reasonable assumptions, and iterate quickly as reality pushes back.
Someone who thinks in terms of systems, not just models. You care about how data is generated, how it flows through systems, how models are deployed, and how outputs actually influence product behavior. Accuracy matters - but so does latency, reliability, and explainability.
An empathetic collaborator. You can work effectively with product, firmware, backend, and design partners - translating between business goals, physical constraints, and technical implementation. You communicate clearly and value shared ownership over individual heroics.


Job Type: Full-time, Permanent

Benefits

Dental Care, Vision Care & Life Insurance premiums fully paid by Jetson

Extended Health Care

Disability Insurance

Education Support

Parental Leave Top Up

Equity

Work Location: In person - Kitchener, ON

Do you feel like you don't have everything that's listed above but can still do the job? If you have the core skills and experience that we’re looking for and are willing to use your talent to learn the rest, we encourage you to apply!

Title: Business Systems Analyst
Description:
In this Business Systems Analyst role, you will assess business processes and user requirements to support the delivery of a SaaS software platform to financial services customers across Canada and the US.




The company operates with team members across Canada, allowing you to work remotely from your home office. And you may occasionally visit their offices in either Vancouver or Toronto, or Canadian customers in other provinces, from time-to-time (e.g. a few times per year max).




You will partner with customers to run discovery sessions, gather requirements, and outline project scope for software implementations. This includes mapping business needs to system functionality and ensuring alignment across solution design, configuration, and testing activities.




This is a full-time position, and includes a competitive base salary, paid vacation and extended health and dental benefits.




A key draw of this role is the opportunity to work closely with a modern SaaS platform in the financial services space, while collaborating with cross-functional technical and delivery teams.

HOW TO APPLY

A bit about myself - my name is Nathan Wawruck, and I am a recruitment consultant based in Vancouver, BC. I have over a decade of matching technology professionals to job opportunities, and I am always looking to connect with like-minded people in the Vancouver area.

If you're interested in pursuing this role, please apply to this posting online, and contact me for an initial discussion. This is a chance for us to discuss specifics, such as the company name, the key hiring managers, and the more in-depth job requirements. This is also a great opportunity to talk about your overall career goals and preferences. We can even discuss other potential roles which may fit what you're looking for. Please find me on LinkedIn by searching my name: Nathan Wawruck




REQUIREMENTS




Here are some of the items that you'll bring to the table for this role:

You have three or more years of SaaS implementation experience
You have experience leading client discovery and documenting business and system requirements
You are a self-starter with a growth mindset who brings strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
You are effective at communicating and collaborating with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders via video meetings, phone calls, email, chat, etc.

Title: Manufacturing Data Analyst
Description:
One of our manufacturing clients in Guelph, ON is looking for Manufacturing Data Analyst for their plant.

Pay - $95K

Responsibilities


Process, clean, and validate data to ensure accuracy and reliability for analysis
Analyze large raw datasets to identify trends and generate meaningful business insights
Develop data-driven solutions using Python or similar scripting languages
Work closely with specialists, project managers, process engineers, and management teams to gather and understand business requirements
Design, develop, and maintain reports, dashboards, metrics, and analytics solutions to support business decisions
Use data mining and analytical techniques to build and maintain customer segmentation and predictive models
Recommend new metrics, reporting methods, and visualization techniques to improve data analysis and reporting efficiency
Support platforms that provide automated and ad-hoc access to large datasets
Assist with IIoT network configuration and troubleshooting in coordination with local and global IT network engineering teams


Qualifications


Bachelor s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Applied AI, or a related field
Coursework focused on statistics, algorithms, relational databases, and advanced calculus
Minimum 3 years of experience in a similar role within the automotive manufacturing industry
Strong understanding of digital transformation initiatives and manufacturing technologies
Solid background in data analysis, artificial intelligence, and machine learning methodologies
Experience with network engineering is considered an asset
Familiarity with IBA measurement systems and software is an asset
Relevant certifications are considered an asset, including:
Certified Data Management Professional (CDMP)
Certified Analytics Professional (CAP)
Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA)

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Established in 1973, Roots is a proudly Canadian lifestyle brand that celebrates authenticity, quality, and community. Inspired by the great outdoors, we create timeless, sustainable apparel and leather goods designed for comfort and everyday adventure. At Roots, we believe in fostering a culture of inclusivity, creativity, and teamwork – where every team member plays a role in shaping our brand’s legacy. If you bring passion to everything you do, lead with integrity, and believe in the power of collaboration to drive growth, we’d love to have you on our journey!

Nature & Scope → Your Role at Roots

As an Analytics Engineer, you'll sit at the intersection of data engineering and analytics, turning raw retail data into trusted, scalable assets and the insights that bring them to life. As a collaborative member of our data team, you'll contribute to the data models that power solutions across the Roots business and help build the data products, semantic layers, and self-service tools that empower every team at Roots to access trusted data independently. Our data team is small and collaborative, meaning you'll wear multiple hats and have broad exposure across the business.

Key Responsibilities → How You’ll Make An Impact


Support end-to-end data transformation, converting raw omnichannel source data (POS, e-commerce, inventory, CRM, and marketing) into clean, structured layers within Snowflake.
Contribute to the design and build of components of the Snowflake data mart, applying dimensional modelling best practices including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions, to help create reliable, performant data products that serve ad hoc analysis, BI dashboards, and AI data solutions.
Support Power BI report development end-to-end, from semantic model design to polished, publish-ready reports, giving commercial, merchandising, marketing, and finance teams self-service access to trusted data without analyst dependency.
Perform ad hoc analysis using SQL and Python directly in Snowflake to help answer pressing business questions, uncover trends, and deliver timely insights that inform planning, buying, and operational decisions.
Assist in maintaining data quality standards, including testing frameworks, pipeline alerting, and documentation, to help ensure the data mart is trustworthy and well-understood across the business.
Support the development and maintenance of Gen AI data solutions, including natural language interfaces enabling any business user to query the data mart in plain English.
Partner cross-functionally with merchandising, planning, marketing, and commercial teams to understand analytical needs and help translate them into scalable data products.
Participate in the continuous improvement of our data platform, contributing ideas around new tools, patterns, and AI capabilities that keep Roots ahead of the curve.


Qualifications & Experience → The Skills You Bring


3+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, business intelligence, or an equivalent data analytics role.
Strong SQL proficiency and hands-on experience with Snowflake or a comparable cloud data warehouse.
Hands-on experience with ELT/ETL platforms, with Matillion or a similar cloud-native transformation tool preferred.
Proficiency building data models and dashboards with Power BI.
Solid understanding of dimensional modelling methodologies including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions.
Experience with version control using Git.
Familiarity with data quality practices including testing, documentation, and pipeline monitoring.
Exposure to Gen AI solutions such as Snowflake Cortex or Microsoft Fabric Copilot is preferred.
Retail or apparel industry experience across omnichannel environments is an asset.
Python proficiency for data transformation, ad hoc analysis, or automation is a plus.


At Roots we appreciate that skills and expertise are cultivated through a range of experiences. We are committed to reflecting Canada's diverse landscape in our products, team, and workplace culture. We value your unique perspective and encourage you to apply, even if you don't meet every listed requirement. Accommodations are available for applicants throughout the recruitment process.

Please note: Roots uses technology-assisted tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of the recruitment process. These tools may be used to help review applications, assess qualifications, and support interview documentation. All hiring decisions are reviewed and made by our Talent team and key leaders involved in the recruitment process.

The posted salary range is intended to reflect the competitive market value of this role and is provided to support transparency in our hiring process. Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to internal equity, relevant skills, demonstrated knowledge, experience, and overall fit for the position.

  • Exposure to Gen AI solutions such as Snowflake Cortex or Microsoft Fabric Copilot is preferred.
  • Retail or apparel industry experience across omnichannel environments is an asset.
  • Python proficiency for data transformation, ad hoc analysis, or automation is a plus.

At Roots we appreciate that skills and expertise are cultivated through a range of experiences. We are committed to reflecting Canada's diverse landscape in our products, team, and workplace culture. We value your unique perspective and encourage you to apply, even if you don't meet every listed requirement. Accommodations are available for applicants throughout the recruitment process.

Please note: Roots uses technology-assisted tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of the recruitment process. These tools may be used to help review applications, assess qualifications, and support interview documentation. All hiring decisions are reviewed and made by our Talent team and key leaders involved in the recruitment process.

The posted salary range is intended to reflect the competitive market value of this role and is provided to support transparency in our hiring process. Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to internal equity, relevant skills, demonstrated knowledge, experience, and overall fit for the position.

Title: Analytics & Insights Specialist
Description:
Why Meridian?

At Meridian our aspiration is to integrate our purpose into everything we do for people, the planet, and communities. We believe that our greatest opportunity is to create opportunity and meet people where they are.

Factor us into your next career opportunity. We want you to grow with us and have an experience that’s different. This is a place where you can expect the unexpected.

Find our story here: About Meridian

The role:

The Analytics & Insights Specialist provides proactive, actionable prescriptive analytical support and business insight to the Lines of Business(LOB). With strong analytical skills, a knowledge of reporting systems, and a talent for influencing without authority, they will act as an internal consultant, adding value and insight to key stakeholders and management.

As a subject matter expert, the Analytics & Insights Specialist demonstrates an ability to present complicated analytical material in a concise and compelling manner and, interpret insights in a meaningful way for management and partners.

The role will support the advancement of analytics and data management, processes, and tools to contribute to the achievement of Meridian’s strategic and in year goals. This role will be responsible for maintaining and enhancing the current Reporting environment along with the development of new and adhoc reporting to support the line of businesses.

Key Responsibilities

Insights, Analytics & Reporting


Analyze, prepares and presents scheduled reports (i.e. key business performance indicators, dashboards and scorecards) and ad hoc reports (i.e. in support of projects or departmental initiatives) for the lines of business, both proactively and upon request
Acts as the analytics lead for Line of business on internal and enterprise initiatives
Provides Line of business leaders with management reporting that identifies issues, opportunities and emerging trends that enables the user to take action
Identifies and monitors key performance indicators, sharing relevant, accurate and insightful findings with LOB leadership team and key partners at various levels within the organization on a timely basis
Leads qualitative and quantitative analysis of relevant industry and consumer behaviour trends to highlight improvements, growth opportunities and new business opportunities
Demonstrates an ability to leverage technology to streamline processes, tools and analysis
Maintain & Enhance current LOB Reporting environment
Development of new reporting & conversion of existing legacy reporting using new technology


Business Solutions


Assists with the development of various programs by collecting, analysing and collating Member information ensuring that Meridian delivers a personalized solution that is in relation to their transactional, advice or price-driven relationships
Provides recommendations to enhance the Member Experience while improving the overall efficiency of its processes
Educate the business on opportunities to enhance Member needs and contribute to relationship growth;


Communication


Coordinates the acquisition of data from various, relevant data sources and applies analytical skills to convert data into meaningful information
Assesses information and conducts research to evaluate business issues
Interprets results and delivers information and/or presentations with impact
Collaborates across all departments within the LOB and establishes strong communication among team members to determine and meet their reporting and analytical needs
Consults with internal stakeholders to develop actionable insights that help to accelerate aspects of the Member Experience and Strategic plans
Effectively demonstrates the ability to explain complex information and concepts to different audiences, including professionals in a manner tailored to the audience and elicits receptivity
Networks with other like-minded individuals to keep abreast of new solutions, resources, and analytical techniques
Builds an external source of information that increases the teams understanding of consumer behaviours, expectations, and current/future attributes


Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


Degree or diploma in mathematics, statistics or computer science or related discipline;
Experience in a financial institution environment with a strong understanding of financial metrics, segmentation and industry drivers.
Proven analytic skills, including mining, evaluation, analysis, and visualization (ability to analyse large datasets)
Technical writing experience in relevant areas, including queries, reports, and presentations
High level of proficiency in designing & developing custom analytics utilizing available or externally obtained data
Strong SQL and Excel skills with the ability to learn other analytic tools
Practical experience in statistical analysis through the use of statistical packages including Excel, Power BI and SAS
Business and partnership focused with an understanding of how Member Experience enables the business
Demonstrates a passion for creative problem-solving and a desire to propose changes that proactively seize market opportunities;
Forward thinking with an openness to change to identify new opportunities
Expertise with research methodologies and research design
Excellent communication and influencing skills
Proven ability to work autonomously in cross-functional teams, with internal customers and key stakeholders
Strong expertise in analysis and fluency with various software and techniques of analysis
Commitment to and drive for continuous improvement
Strong process management skills, initiative, ability to manage competing priorities and attention to detail are critical
Exhibits an analytical, creative, and innovative approach to meeting stakeholder and business needs
Able to handle confidential and proprietary information with discretion
Demonstrated partnership/service skills to effectively provide consultation to the Member Experience team
Member Focus - Personally demonstrating that our Members are our highest priority.
Proactive - Seize the initiative by anticipating needs and identifying opportunities to provide a higher level of service.
Open Communication - Engaging in honest and transparent dialogue to develop mutual understanding, trust and cultivate deeper relationships.
Business Savvy - Continuously acquiring the knowledge needed to do one’s job to the fullest, using internal or external resources and applying the knowledge productively.
Partner Orientation - Initiates opportunities for collaboration and maintains productive relationships to achieve shared goals.
Integrity - Doing the right thing by always acting with openness, honesty and respect.
Talent Developer: nurtures and supports direct reports, identifies strong team members, develops digital and analytical skills in direct reports
Creates a Supportive and Positive Team Environment: creates and contributes to a positive team environment which encourages innovation, risk taking, and learning; speeds up team learning through sharing best practices; leverages the strengths and opportunities of the broader marketing team
Knowledge of Fabric will be an asset.


This role represents an existing vacancy. Internal candidates are being considered as part of our standard process.

Salary Range: CAD $68,800 - $103,200 annually

Compensation for this role is based on a combination of skills, experience, and internal equity. Candidates with stronger alignment to the role’s requirements may be placed higher within the range. Your recruiter will share more information about our total rewards package during the hiring process.

Office Location: Toronto Corporate Office located at 3280 Bloor St W, Etobicoke, ON or St. Catharines Corporate Office, located at 75 Corporate Park Drive, St. Catharines, ON.

Meridian has a remote work policy that allows flexibility for employees to work remotely but also requires a minimum of one day per week in the office for purposeful meetings to collaborate, innovate and build effective relationships with your team, your colleagues and your leader which is important to us.

What's in it for you?


We have an inclusive and collaborative working environment that encourages teamwork, creativity, curiosity, and celebrates success!
We provide you with the tools and technology needed to delight your candidates and clients!
You will get to work with and learn from diverse industry leaders and colleagues, who have hailed from top organizations.
Hybrid work arrangements with in-person office time to collaborate, innovate and build relationships with your colleagues.
This is not your typical "corporate" job. We work hard and we have fun!


Build the Future of Banking with Us

At Meridian, we’re not just adapting to the future—we’re shaping it. We see talent as a catalyst for sustainable growth, community impact, and future-readiness. As the financial landscape evolves, we’re looking for team members who bring:


Embedding & leveraging technology – Confidence and willingness to learn and integrate AI and emerging technologies into new ways of working. Experience with low/no code tools like the Microsoft Power Platform and generative AI productivity tools like Microsoft Copilot is considered an asset
Critical Thinking – The ability to analyze, evaluate and interpret information from various sources and apply logic and problem solving to make decisions.
Data Driven– Leverage data and insights to identify, prioritize and improve member experience and business efficiency.
Curiosity & Flexibility – Comfortable navigating and embracing change and uncertainty, ask more questions, explore more options. Continuously learn and grow.


Who we are:

Meridian is Ontario’s largest credit union, and second largest in Canada, helping to grow the lives of our more than 380,000 Members. Meridian has more than 80 years of banking history and is 100% owned by its members. With 87 retail branches and 15 Business Banking Centers across Ontario and $ 32B in assets under management, Meridian offers a full range of financial products and services to its retail, business banking and wealth members. With over 2200 employees and corporate offices located in Toronto and St. Catharines, Meridian has a track record of creating and delivering innovative new offerings and is committed to investing in the communities that we serve. Our plan is to build on our momentum as we deliver on our purpose - helping our members achieve their best life.

Find our story here: About Meridian

Experience the Difference!

Meridian committed to promoting an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected and supported. We are dedicated to employing a workforce that reflects the diversity of our communities and Members in which we live and serve.

Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, or disability.

Please note that due to the volume of applications, only those under consideration will be contacted for an interview.

Thank you for your interest in Meridian Credit Union.

Follow us on Twitter at

@MeridianCareers

Connect with us on LinkedIn

Meridian only posts jobs on our official Careers Site, LinkedIn, and Indeed. If you find a Meridian job posting on any other site, please verify it by ensuring it directs back to our official career page. Meridian never asks candidates to apply through third‑party links that do not redirect to our Careers portal (UltiPro). For more tips on how to protect yourself from job scams visit Protect yourself from job scams | Meridian.

Title: Oakville Fire Data Analyst
Description:
Job Details

Permanent Full Time (CUPE 1329)

Posting Status

Open to all current Town of Oakville employees and external applicants

Closing Date

Applications for this position must be received at by no later than 11:59pm on May 31, 2026.

This job posting is for an existing vacancy and therefore will be filled accordingly.

Job Responsibilities

What gets measured, gets managed. The incumbent is a critical resource in our relentless pursuit of continuous improvement by ensuring the end-to-end journey is tracked, measured, and reported on. Key performance indicators that measure the health of service delivery efforts need to be consistently reported on and continuously improved upon as department requirements change.

Reporting to the Supervisor, Administrative Services, the data analyst is a key contributor to operational efficiency and effectiveness by analyzing complex structured and unstructured datasets to help guide business decisions. The incumbent will use a variety of analytical techniques to prepare, visualize, analyze, and communicate key performance measures that drive business decisions across the Oakville Fire Department, partner business units and various levels of government.

The incumbent will work in collaboration with the fire department leadership, operations teams, IT staff and external stakeholders to turn data into insights that can be used to make sound organizational business decisions and inform policy development.

The analyst provides core application data support for all supported departments by ensuring data is created and structured in a format that will provide management across the organization with insights to help in key decision making for their services.

Qualifications


Completion of a three-year Diploma or Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Information Management, or similar analytical disciplines.
A minimum of 3 years of experience in data analysis and reporting is an asset.
Experience soliciting and documenting client requirements and producing analyses, reports, and data visualizations based on the stated needs.
Experience using data to illuminate and solve problems.
Ability to communicate complex analysis in a clear manner for a variety of audiences.
Experience using data analysis tools such as Qlikview, MS Power BI, Google Analytics, Tableau, ArcGIS, FDM, First Due and Telestaff.
Ability to adapt to new software programs with administrative rights.
Experience working on data analytic projects in the areas of social networks, website analytics, with project performance measures and established KPI’s.
Strong understanding of compliance reporting to Emergency Management Ontario, Office of the Fire Marshal, and/or other legislative practices is considered an asset.
Experience working with project teams and all levels of staff.
Data Management Association (DAMA) Certified Data Management Professional (CDMP) or The Data Warehouse Institute (TDWI) Certified Business Intelligence Professional (CBIP) are considered an asset.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, with the ability to understand complex inter-departmental processes to identify key performance measures.
Create and understand data structures, data management theories, principles and practices.
Strong understanding of the tasks associated with and challenges of working with unstructured data.
Competency with creation of data queries and data extraction methods and tools.
Ability to quickly understand end-user needs and requirements and the organizational goals and objectives.
Able to articulate complex data concepts to non-technical audiences.
Excellent oral and written communication, and presentation skills.
Direct experience and strong understanding of statistical research, data management, reporting, analysis, trends and forecasting.
Exceptional computer skills, including knowledge of Visio, SharePoint, Microsoft Office.
Knowledge of systems including Salesforce CRM, Mitel IVR, JDE Enterprise, Amanda, Google Analytics. ESRI GIS, and CityWorks is an asset.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Access and Excel including the design and maintenance of spreadsheets/databases/software programs.
Strong time-management skills.
Understanding of confidentiality requirements.
Strong team and customer-oriented focus.
Works independently, self-motivated and proactive.


DATED: May 11, 2026

The Town’s recruitment software includes elements of artificial intelligence to assist in the screening and short-listing of qualified candidates.

This job profile reflects the general requirements necessary to perform the principal functions of the job. This does not include all of the work requirements of the job. Applicants are required to demonstrate through their application and in the interview process that their qualifications match those specified. The minimum threshold score for the interview is 75%.

We thank all applicants and advise that only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Who are we?

Cheil Worldwide is the 11th-largest creative agency in the world. Headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, Cheil was founded by Samsung in 1973 to grow its local and global business. We are driven by our passion to create mind-blowing ideas, technologies, and one-of-a-kind experiences by connecting advertising with digital; online and offline; data and events; and consumers and brands. Cheil North America has offices in Toronto, NYC and Dallas, and serves as the lead agency for all Samsung projects across the continent. Collectively, we are a unified force of creative problem-solvers who share a passion for developing ground-breaking ideas rooted in data, driven by insights, and enabled by innovative technology

Position Summary

Samsung.com is scaling its experimentation and insights practice. We need a data-driven analyst who can run A/B tests, govern data quality, and turn behavior patterns into clear, revenue-growing recommendations across every division (Mobile & Wearables, Home Entertainment, Digital Appliances, B2B).

Key Responsibilities


Plan, launch, and analyze A/B and multivariate tests in Adobe Target and Insider—defining hypotheses, sizing audiences, monitoring in-flight results, and presenting statistically sound read-outs.
Mine GA4, Adobe Analytics, and CDP datasets to uncover behavioral insights, segment performance (device, traffic source, campaign, division), and translate findings into prioritized optimization recommendations.
Design and maintain self-serve dashboards in Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI, automating data pipelines and enforcing metric definitions for stakeholders from squad level to executive.
Own Google Tag Manager configurations—creating events, enhanced-e-commerce tracking, and custom dimensions, performing end-to-end QA in dev and prod.
Define, document, and manage the GA4-compliant data layer that powers analytics, Adobe Target, and Insider tests, ensuring consistency, privacy compliance, and future scalability.
Collaborate cross-functionally with UX, Merchandising, Development, and Legal teams to ensure experimentation and tagging requirements are embedded in every release.
Champion data quality and governance by establishing validation checklists, monitoring discrepancies, and educating stakeholders on best practices.



Must-Have Qualifications


3–5 years in digital analytics, CRO, or experimentation for a high-traffic e-commerce site.
A/B testing experience with Adobe Target and/or Insider (or similar).
Advanced use of GA4 and Adobe Analytics—including explorations, calculated metrics, and segmentation.
Demonstrated data-layer management for GA4 (schema design, mapping, validation).
SQL/BigQuery proficiency and strong Excel/Sheets skills.
Dashboard development in Looker Studio / Tableau / Power BI.
Deep familiarity with GTM, tag-debugging tools, and browser/network debuggers.
Ability to explain statistical concepts (confidence, lift, p-values) and craft persuasive narratives for non-analysts.
Bridge the gap between artistic creative and hard performance data to ensure digital campaigns are built for conversion, not just engagement



DESIRED


Adobe RT-CDP Mastery: Experience leveraging Real-Time Customer Data Platforms to unify cross-channel data into actionable profiles for 1:1 personalization.
Omnichannel Personalization: Experience using Adobe Target or Insider to deliver personalized experiences across Web, Mobile (Samsung Members App), and CRM channels.



Nice-to-Have Extras


Server-side testing, Adobe Launch, or Tealium iQ experience.
Working knowledge of HTML/CSS/JavaScript for front-end troubleshooting.
Exposure to SEO, paid-media attribution, or product-recommendation engines.
Background in consumer electronics or retail e-commerce.



What Happens After You Apply

Application review: Our Human Resources Manager reviews every resume. We get upwards of 100+ applications for some roles, it can take a few days, if your resume is shortlisted, we'll be sure to reach out. We thank you for your patience!

Salary range: $70,000 - $75,000 CAD annually

Final compensation for this role will be determined by various factors such as a candidate’s relevant work experience, skills, and certifications

Work Arrangement:

This role is a fixed-term contract for a period of one (1) year, with the possibility of extension based on business needs and performance.

While this position follows a hybrid work model, with three (3) days in-office (Tuesday through Thursday) and two (2) days work-from-home (Monday and Friday), during business requirements, needs, client deadlines, you may be required to be in office on all 5 days.

Company Address: #100, 6790 Century Avenue, Mississauga, L5N 2V8

Who are we?

Cheil Worldwide is the 11th-largest creative agency in the world. Headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, Cheil was founded by Samsung in 1973 to grow its local and global business. We are driven by our passion to create mind-blowing ideas, technologies, and one-of-a-kind experiences by connecting advertising with digital; online and offline; data and events; and consumers and brands. Cheil North America has offices in Toronto, NYC and Dallas, and serves as the lead agency for all Samsung projects across the continent. Collectively, we are a unified force of creative problem-solvers who share a passion for developing ground-breaking ideas rooted in data, driven by insights, and enabled by innovative technology

Position Summary

Samsung.com is scaling its experimentation and insights practice. We need a data-driven analyst who can run A/B tests, govern data quality, and turn behavior patterns into clear, revenue-growing recommendations across every division (Mobile & Wearables, Home Entertainment, Digital Appliances, B2B).

Key Responsibilities

  • Plan, launch, and analyze A/B and multivariate tests in Adobe Target and Insider—defining hypotheses, sizing audiences, monitoring in-flight results, and presenting statistically sound read-outs.
  • Mine GA4, Adobe Analytics, and CDP datasets to uncover behavioral insights, segment performance (device, traffic source, campaign, division), and translate findings into prioritized optimization recommendations.
  • Design and maintain self-serve dashboards in Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI, automating data pipelines and enforcing metric definitions for stakeholders from squad level to executive.
  • Own Google Tag Manager configurations—creating events, enhanced-e-commerce tracking, and custom dimensions, performing end-to-end QA in dev and prod.
  • Define, document, and manage the GA4-compliant data layer that powers analytics, Adobe Target, and Insider tests, ensuring consistency, privacy compliance, and future scalability.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with UX, Merchandising, Development, and Legal teams to ensure experimentation and tagging requirements are embedded in every release.
  • Champion data quality and governance by establishing validation checklists, monitoring discrepancies, and educating stakeholders on best practices.


Must-Have Qualifications

  • 3–5 years in digital analytics, CRO, or experimentation for a high-traffic e-commerce site.
  • A/B testing experience with Adobe Target and/or Insider (or similar).
  • Advanced use of GA4 and Adobe Analytics—including explorations, calculated metrics, and segmentation.
  • Demonstrated data-layer management for GA4 (schema design, mapping, validation).
  • SQL/BigQuery proficiency and strong Excel/Sheets skills.
  • Dashboard development in Looker Studio / Tableau / Power BI.
  • Deep familiarity with GTM, tag-debugging tools, and browser/network debuggers.
  • Ability to explain statistical concepts (confidence, lift, p-values) and craft persuasive narratives for non-analysts.
  • Bridge the gap between artistic creative and hard performance data to ensure digital campaigns are built for conversion, not just engagement


DESIRED

  • Adobe RT-CDP Mastery: Experience leveraging Real-Time Customer Data Platforms to unify cross-channel data into actionable profiles for 1:1 personalization.
  • Omnichannel Personalization: Experience using Adobe Target or Insider to deliver personalized experiences across Web, Mobile (Samsung Members App), and CRM channels.


Nice-to-Have Extras

  • Server-side testing, Adobe Launch, or Tealium iQ experience.
  • Working knowledge of HTML/CSS/JavaScript for front-end troubleshooting.
  • Exposure to SEO, paid-media attribution, or product-recommendation engines.
  • Background in consumer electronics or retail e-commerce.


What Happens After You Apply

Application review: Our Human Resources Manager reviews every resume. We get upwards of 100+ applications for some roles, it can take a few days, if your resume is shortlisted, we'll be sure to reach out. We thank you for your patience!

Salary range: $70,000 - $75,000 CAD annually

Final compensation for this role will be determined by various factors such as a candidate’s relevant work experience, skills, and certifications

Work Arrangement:

This role is a fixed-term contract for a period of one (1) year, with the possibility of extension based on business needs and performance.

While this position follows a hybrid work model, with three (3) days in-office (Tuesday through Thursday) and two (2) days work-from-home (Monday and Friday), during business requirements, needs, client deadlines, you may be required to be in office on all 5 days.

Company Address: #100, 6790 Century Avenue, Mississauga, L5N 2V8

Title: Data Specialist, Assessment
Description:
Job description:



A day in the life of a Data Specialist at Esri Canada:

Design, develop, test, and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines and data integration processes to support analytics, modeling, and product features.
Build and optimize data models, data stores, and transformation workflows to ensure data performance, accuracy, and maintainability.
Collect, ingest, clean, and integrate large volumes of structured and unstructured data from multiple internal and external sources, ensuring data quality, integrity, and analytical readiness.
Collaborate with software developers and platform teams to integrate data pipelines and analytics capabilities into production systems and enterprise software solutions.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and improve data pipeline performance, reliability, and scalability, contributing to operational stability and continuous improvement.
Develop, evaluate, and refine descriptive, predictive, and diagnostic models using statistical and machine learning techniques such as regression, classification, clustering, and time series analysis.
Translate analytical and modeling outputs into clear, actionable insights through data visualization, dashboards, reports, and presentations tailored to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Support the operationalization of analytical and predictive models, including deployment, monitoring, and ongoing maintenance in production environments.
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives by researching, evaluating, and applying emerging data engineering, analytics, and data science tools, technologies, and best practices.

General Responsibilities:

Participate collaboratively in the company Employee Development Program.
Communicate effectively with internal and external personnel at all levels representing the company in a professional manner at all times.
Actively participate in company, staff or individual one-on-one meetings on an ongoing basis for the purposes of effective teamwork, enhanced communication and progressive co-operation strategies within the company as well as other departments across the company.

Title: Business Operations & Systems Analyst
Description:
Business Analyst

Location: Cambridge, Ontario

Company: Summit Water

Reports to: CEO initially; dotted-line support to Operations, Sales, Finance, and Engineering




Role Overview

Summit Water is seeking a practical, detail-oriented Business Operations & Systems Analyst to help improve how we run the business. This is a cross-functional role focused on reporting, data cleanup, process documentation, and better use of our ERP, CRM, and internal systems.

The ideal candidate is analytical, organized, comfortable working with imperfect data, and willing to work hands-on with sales, operations, finance, engineering, and service teams.




Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain weekly reports on sales, quotes, orders, margins, inventory, backlog, and pipeline activity.
Clean up and standardize business data, including part numbers, item descriptions, pricing files, customer records, vendor records, and inventory data.
Support better use of ERP, CRM, SharePoint, and other internal systems.
Document standard operating procedures for key business processes, including quote-to-order, order entry, inventory, production handoff, service, purchasing, and CRM usage.
Assist with pricing analysis, margin analysis, supplier/customer data, inventory analysis, and product/category reporting.
Support special projects, including acquisition integration, systems improvements, AI initiatives, and operational improvement projects.
Work with department leaders to identify process gaps and recommend practical improvements.




Education & Experience

Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in Business, Operations Management, Supply Chain, Industrial Engineering, Accounting/Finance, Business Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field.
3–7 years of experience in business analysis, operations analysis, sales operations, ERP/CRM support, supply chain, finance, or process improvement.
Experience in distribution, manufacturing, industrial products, service, or project-based businesses is an asset.
Equivalent hands-on experience will be considered for candidates with strong practical business systems, reporting, and process improvement skills.




Skills & Attributes

Strong Excel skills; experience with reporting tools, ERP systems, CRM systems, or databases is an asset.
Comfortable working with messy or incomplete data and turning it into useful business information.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
Practical problem-solver who can improve processes without overcomplicating them.
Good communication skills and ability to work across departments.
Comfortable documenting processes and helping teams follow standard procedures.
Curious, hands-on, and willing to roll up your sleeves.




Ideal Candidate

You are not just a spreadsheet person. You enjoy understanding how a business actually works, cleaning up messy processes, improving systems, and helping people make better decisions. You are practical, organized, and comfortable working in a growing business where not everything is perfectly documented yet.




Compensation

Competitive salary based on experience. Anticipated range: $85,000–$105,000, with flexibility for an exceptional candidate.

Title: Job ID #31971: Fleet Quality Coordinator & Data Analyst
Description:
Contribute to the City of Hamilton, one of Canada’s largest cities - home to a diverse and strong economy, an active and inclusive community, a robust cultural and dining scene, hundreds of kilometers of hiking trails and natural beauty just minutes from the downtown core, and so much more. Join our diverse team of talented and ambitious staff who embody our values of sensational service, courageous change, steadfast integrity, collective ownership and being engaged empowered employees. Help us achieve our vision of being the best place to raise a child and age successfully. #BeTheReason


Job ID #31971: Fleet Quality Coordinator & Data Analyst
Union: Non-Union
Job Description ID #: 7662
Close date: Interested applicants please submit your application online at www.hamilton.ca/city-council/jobs-city by 4:00 p.m. on May 27, 2026.
Vacancy type: This posting is for an existing vacancy
Internal applicants should apply with your work e-mail address. External applicants are considered only after the internal posting process has been completed. Only applicants chosen for an interview will be contacted.


Summary Of Duties

Reporting to the Fleet Manager. The Fleet Quality Coordinator & Data Analyst will be responsible for fleet continuous improvement initiatives, developing performance measurement, as well as quality management. The coordinator will specialize in administrative, analytical, and technical work related to identification, evaluation, prioritization, and implementation of strategic initiatives.

This position provides support to Fleet Services in continuous improvement initiatives that drive efficiencies such as: green fleet/new technologies, vehicle maintenance management scheduling, operational and business process reviews, provides support and direction in the development of standards and methodologies to improve processes and efficiencies.

Using a "best practices" approach, this position develops and delivers quality service in a timely and cost -effective manner. This position will work with the Manager to establish, monitor and report on departmental KPI's and metrics.

GENERAL DUTIES

Lead and supports the development, implementation, and management of a Fleet wide process improvement program by providing insight and guidance.

Lead and supports the development, implementation and ongoing updating of performance metrics for all Fleet Services operational support areas.

Lead and supports the development and implementation of continuous improvement strategies to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of service delivery.

Use statistical analysis to identify performance trends and make recommendations for project opportunities/enhanced services.

Leads and supports specific projects and investigative assignments such as, green technology upgrades, process improvements, change management or implementation of policy/procedure requirements, cost-benefit analyses, benchmarking studies.

Performs analysis on various Fleet related data including but not limited to vehicle and equipment classification, utilization, maintenance activities, life cycle and green technology initiatives, vehicle recharging and fuel site infrastructure.

Uses statistical analysis to make recommendations for business opportunities, enhanced services, process improvements, Grant funding programs and implementation.

Interacts with consultants on Departmental projects overseeing the collection of data and analysis and reviewing/commenting

Coordinates internal and external data for Municipal Benchmarking such as MBN Canada or other corporate benchmarking.

Involved in business process reviews, improvement initiatives, provides strategic and process improvement advice to support business operations.

Makes recommendations for improvements to processes, including efficiency improvements, modification/elimination/development of tools, and the introduction and use of technology.

Recommends related monitoring KPI's through maintenance management system to determine strengths and needs of staff, to identify training needs and goal setting measures to reach optimal performance levels. Includes oversight of development of performance indicator tools.

Synthesizes analytical outputs and materials to produce reports. Creates and contributes to reports for internal and external use (e.g., Council reports, memorandums, website material/open data) to communicate performance, financial results, staffing levels and other related activities for the purpose of sharing with internal and/or external persons/groups.

Assist in developing project charters, scope, deliverables, timelines, resource requirements, and identify project risks. Update City’s internal share point system with continuous improvement projects.

Works with the Manager, to complete required applications to secure funding through outside source such as Government grant funding opportunities.

Responsible for project plan development and project resource planning along with the identification of project task. Identifies project resources and skill requirements and estimates project and sustainability costs while overseeing the project schedule, milestones and project resources.

Analyzes feedback from Departmental pilot groups and stakeholders

Prepares oral and written presentations and reports regularly on the status of projects, milestone achievement, fiscal status, change and risk management. Prepares recommendations and outlines preferred course of action.

Compiles and analysis information from various sources and reports on Return on Investment for new fleet technologies (fuel, hybrid, leasing).

Compile data from various sources(internal-external) and Fleet programs and databases including but not limited to Hexagon EAM, Hansen, Access, and Excel and produces comprehensive reports.

Investigates, develops, recommends and implements policies and procedures and creates a supportive work environment. Recommends and makes changes to Fleet programs.

Assist and leads development and maintenance of Standard Operating Procedures that enhance organizational effectiveness and support training programs that provide optimal service functions to effectively transfer knowledge to all levels of users.

Audits data entered in Fleet systems to ensure accuracy according to stated acceptable formats, policy and procedures (extended use, rebuilds, utilization).

Perform checks, reconciliations, comparisons and verification of transactions and other matters.

Review documents, records, processes and reports for accuracy, effectiveness and reliability (projected fuel savings, Idling, return on disposal).

Provide analysis on fleet repair costs by work types, cost group, vehicle and class codes.

Act as a key user to identify user needs and implement system upgrades.

Perform such other duties as may be assigned, which are directly related to the job function.

Qualifications


Demonstrated experience in the above duties normally acquired at the post secondary level of education through the completion of Finance, Business Administration or Materials Management program, or a combination of education and relevant work experience.
Computer literacy and proficiency utilizing Microsoft products (Word, Excel, Power Point and Outlook), and other software programs which may be required by the Fleet Services Division.
Demonstrated skills in analytical and conceptual thinking, statistical, planning and evaluation, forecasting and financial skills.
Ability to transform process and policy recommendations into implementation strategy plans.
Proven ability to review and analyse program data to improve quality assurance and further develop continuous improvement strategies.
Demonstrated experience and skill to input and manipulate data accurately at an acceptable Intermediate speed. Able to create statistical reports and graphs by utilizing different resources.
Practical experience in process improvement or quality management using Lean Six Sigma or similar methodology would be considered an asset.
Ability to perform process reviews without detailed instruction and supervision and organize and follow through with plans and solve problems. Demonstrated ability to develop suggestions through the application of innovative and creative thinking, analytical skills and sound reasoning.
Demonstrated knowledge of Procurement processes
Demonstrated knowledge of automotive industry would be considered an asset.
Knowledge of maintenance management system such as Hexagon EAM software programs would be an asset.


THE INCUMBENT SHALL COMPLY WITH ALL HEALTH AND SAFETY POLICIES AND PRACTICES FOR THIS POSITION AND THE WORKPLACE.

SALARY:

Salary Grade 5

HOURS:

35 per week

Title: Data Analyst, People Analytics
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

The People Analytics team at Lyft exists to build data trust — turning people data into clear, reliable insights that help our HR Business Partners, talent leaders, and executives make better decisions. We're a small, high-impact team in the middle of a meaningful BI transformation: we recently moved to ThoughtSpot as our primary analytics platform, and we're not just replicating old dashboards — we're rethinking what self-service analytics looks like for a People team. That includes exploring ThoughtSpot's AI capabilities to surface proactive insights, enable natural language querying, and reduce the friction between a business question and a data answer. There's real greenfield work here, and we're looking for someone who wants to help define what AI-powered people analytics looks like at Lyft.

We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our People Analytics team, based in Toronto. You'll report to the Senior Manager, People Analytics and serve as the first point of contact for incoming data requests across the People organization — triaging, scoping, and delivering on analytical needs ranging from quick ad-hoc pulls to fully built ThoughtSpot dashboards.

This role is well-timed: you'll be joining as we complete our migration from Tableau to ThoughtSpot, which means you'll have genuine influence over how we build our reporting layer and push into ThoughtSpot's AI features — think natural language search, AI-generated insights, and proactive anomaly detection applied to people data. This isn't a "maintain the dashboard" role. It's a chance to help build something new. You'll be a self-starter who is equally comfortable writing SQL and presenting findings to a VP.

Responsibilities:


Serve as the first point of contact for incoming data requests from across the People organization — scoping needs, setting expectations, and delivering high-quality analytical outputs
Build and maintain ThoughtSpot dashboards and liveboards for a broad stakeholder audience including recruiters, People Team members, HRBPs, managers, and executives
Explore and operationalize ThoughtSpot's AI capabilities — including Spotter and AI Highlights — to surface proactive insights for People stakeholders who may not know what question to ask yet
Build and administer survey programs in Qualtrics — including survey design, distribution logic, and close-out — and translate results into dashboards and ad-hoc analyses that inform People team decisions
Partner with Talent and People team leads to define key metrics and help them tell meaningful stories with their data
Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable insights for both People leadership and senior executives
Communicate fluently across technical and non-technical audiences — from SQL-level data discussions to exec-facing summaries
Collaborate with systems and operations partners to improve upstream data quality and enable better downstream analytics
Pull together ad-hoc datasets from disparate sources to answer discrete analytical questions quickly and accurately


Experience:


4+ years of experience in data or business analytics
3+ years of SQL experience, with comfort pulling from complex, multi-source data environments
Experience with ThoughtSpot or a comparable modern BI platform; enthusiasm for AI-assisted analytics and a curiosity about what those tools unlock for people data specifically
Familiarity with Workday or a comparable HCM (understanding the business processes and data model, not just surface-level reporting)
Familiarity with Greenhouse or a comparable ATS preferred
Experience with Qualtrics or a comparable survey platform, including hands-on program administration and the ability to move from raw survey data to a clean, stakeholder-ready analysis
Experience building and maintaining analytical models that others rely on
Understanding of the nuances of people and recruiting data — org structures, headcount definitions, privacy considerations, and the ways these datasets can surprise you


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits


Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. HTML:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

The People Analytics team at Lyft exists to build data trust — turning people data into clear, reliable insights that help our HR Business Partners, talent leaders, and executives make better decisions. We're a small, high-impact team in the middle of a meaningful BI transformation: we recently moved to ThoughtSpot as our primary analytics platform, and we're not just replicating old dashboards — we're rethinking what self-service analytics looks like for a People team. That includes exploring ThoughtSpot's AI capabilities to surface proactive insights, enable natural language querying, and reduce the friction between a business question and a data answer. There's real greenfield work here, and we're looking for someone who wants to help define what AI-powered people analytics looks like at Lyft.

We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our People Analytics team, based in Toronto. You'll report to the Senior Manager, People Analytics and serve as the first point of contact for incoming data requests across the People organization — triaging, scoping, and delivering on analytical needs ranging from quick ad-hoc pulls to fully built ThoughtSpot dashboards.

This role is well-timed: you'll be joining as we complete our migration from Tableau to ThoughtSpot, which means you'll have genuine influence over how we build our reporting layer and push into ThoughtSpot's AI features — think natural language search, AI-generated insights, and proactive anomaly detection applied to people data. This isn't a "maintain the dashboard" role. It's a chance to help build something new. You'll be a self-starter who is equally comfortable writing SQL and presenting findings to a VP.

Responsibilities:

  • Serve as the first point of contact for incoming data requests from across the People organization — scoping needs, setting expectations, and delivering high-quality analytical outputs
  • Build and maintain ThoughtSpot dashboards and liveboards for a broad stakeholder audience including recruiters, People Team members, HRBPs, managers, and executives
  • Explore and operationalize ThoughtSpot's AI capabilities — including Spotter and AI Highlights — to surface proactive insights for People stakeholders who may not know what question to ask yet
  • Build and administer survey programs in Qualtrics — including survey design, distribution logic, and close-out — and translate results into dashboards and ad-hoc analyses that inform People team decisions
  • Partner with Talent and People team leads to define key metrics and help them tell meaningful stories with their data
  • Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable insights for both People leadership and senior executives
  • Communicate fluently across technical and non-technical audiences — from SQL-level data discussions to exec-facing summaries
  • Collaborate with systems and operations partners to improve upstream data quality and enable better downstream analytics
  • Pull together ad-hoc datasets from disparate sources to answer discrete analytical questions quickly and accurately

Experience:

  • 4+ years of experience in data or business analytics
  • 3+ years of SQL experience, with comfort pulling from complex, multi-source data environments
  • Experience with ThoughtSpot or a comparable modern BI platform; enthusiasm for AI-assisted analytics and a curiosity about what those tools unlock for people data specifically
  • Familiarity with Workday or a comparable HCM (understanding the business processes and data model, not just surface-level reporting)
  • Familiarity with Greenhouse or a comparable ATS preferred
  • Experience with Qualtrics or a comparable survey platform, including hands-on program administration and the ability to move from raw survey data to a clean, stakeholder-ready analysis
  • Experience building and maintaining analytical models that others rely on
  • Understanding of the nuances of people and recruiting data — org structures, headcount definitions, privacy considerations, and the ways these datasets can surprise you

Benefits:

  • Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
  • Mental health benefits
  • Family building benefits
  • Child care and pet benefits
  • Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
  • RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
  • In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
  • Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
  • Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays.
