Title: Data Analyst
Description:
You are as unique as your background, experience and point of view. Here, you’ll be encouraged, empowered and challenged to be your best self. You'll work with dynamic colleagues - experts in their fields - who are eager to share their knowledge with you. Your leaders will inspire and help you reach your potential and soar to new heights. Every day, you'll have new and exciting opportunities to make life brighter for our Clients - who are at the heart of everything we do. Discover how you can make a difference in the lives of individuals, families and communities around the world.

Job Description:

Do you thrive at the intersection of business and technology? Do you have the communication skills to bridge technical and non-technical stakeholders? If so, consider a career in the Health Analytics team at Sun Life.

This role is pivotal in connecting what our business needs with what our technology can deliver. You'll work closely with business leaders to understand their goals, then partner with IT teams to bring those solutions to life. Your ability to simplify complexity and eliminate unnecessary steps will directly empower employees across the organization—giving them more time to focus on what truly matters. Beyond the immediate impact, this role offers opportunities to deepen your expertise in emerging technologies, positioning you for continued growth and advancement in an increasingly digital landscape.

Reporting to Senior Data Engineer, the Data Analyst is responsible for developing automation solutions and supporting data engineering best practices of our Health Analytics department.

What will you do?


Transform ad-hoc data requests to automated, self serve solutions.
Reverse engineer legacy reports, collaborate with business to improve them and automate by data pipelines in the cloud.
Support data engineering best practices.
Identify and participate in initiatives for process automation and optimization
Develop innovative solutions for business challenges using critical thinking and problem-solving skills
Own, create, and maintain documentation to support knowledge sharing and ensure appropriate backup coverage


What do you need to succeed?


Technical skills:
Strong proficiency in SQL including complex joins, window functions.
Experience with Python for tooling, and workflow development.
Practical experience with AWS (lambda functions, glue jobs, step functions, CloudWatch etc.) or equivalent cloud platforms.
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts, ETL patterns.
Proficiency with Git and version control workflows.
Detail-oriented: able to follow process notes, carefully preserve data integrity and ensure quality output with minimal to no errors/issues
Communication skills: excellent collaboration with strong written and verbal communication skills; ability to document processes
Takes initiative: resourceful at problem solving and continually improving job processes and procedures. Ability to work independently, as well as part of a team
Adaptability: ability to adapt to changing priorities and manage time effectively to handle multiple tasks concurrently


What will be nice to have:


Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines.
Familiarity with Spark data frames, Spark SQL, PySpark
Exposure to BI tools (e.g., Tableau, QuickSight, Power BI).
Experience using GenAI tools to improve productivity in SQL development, data exploration, and documentation


What’s in it for you?


We’re proud to be a Best Workplace for Hybrid Work by Great Place to Work® Canada
Be part of our continuous improvement journey in developing the next greatest digital enterprise experience that is united by our Purpose: to help Clients and Employees achieve lifetime financial security and live healthier lives.
Flexible Benefits from the day you join to meet the needs of you and your family.
Pension, stock and savings programs to help build and enhance your future financial security.


The Base Pay range is for the primary location for which the job is posted. It may vary depending on the work location of the successful candidate or other factors. In addition to Base Pay, eligible Sun Life employees participate in various incentive plans, payment under which is discretionary and subject to individual and company performance. Certain sales focused roles have sales incentive plans based on individual or group sales results.

Diversity and inclusion have always been at the core of our values at Sun Life. A diverse workforce with wide perspectives and creative ideas benefits our Clients, the communities where we operate and all of us as colleagues. We welcome applications from qualified individuals from all backgrounds.

Persons with disabilities who need accommodation in the application process, or those needing job postings in an alternative format, may e-mail a request to thebrightside@sunlife.com.

We are proud to be a hybrid organization that offers our employees the choice and flexibility to work from both the office and virtually based on the needs of the business, our Clients and you! Several work options are available and can be discussed throughout the selection process depending on the role requirements and individual needs.

We may use artificial intelligence to support candidate sourcing, screening, interview scheduling.

We thank all applicants for showing an interest in this position. Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Salary Range:

60,000/60 000 - 97,000/97 000

Job Category:

Advanced Analytics

29/05/2026

Title: GIS Data and Analytics Analyst Job Details | Richmond Hill
Description:
GIS Data and Analytics Analyst

Posting Id 3341

Department Corporate and Financial Services

Division Information Technology

Section Geospatial Information System (GIS)

Job Grade SEA35 Grade 06

Rate of Pay $84,045.00 - $98,882.00 Annual

Job Type Permanent Full Time

Employee Group SEA

Replacement/New Position Replacement for Existing Vacancy

Posting Type Internal and External

Posting Date 05/11/2026

Application Deadline 05/25/2026

Position Summary

We're looking for a passionate and experienced individual to join our dynamic GIS team within the City of Richmond Hill’s IT Division. You’ll play a key role in implementing the City’s GIS Strategy elements, support data driven decision making across the organization, and promote corporate data governance. You will participate in GIS Platform administration, deliver spatially enabled analytics, automation, and applications that support service optimization.

Highlights Of What You Will Do


Administer GIS Databases & Esri’s Platforms: Enterprise and ArcGIS Online
Manage Process & Product Automations
Design, develop, and maintain automated spatial data workflows using Python, FME, APIs, and ETL processes
Manage Field Tools and Web Map Applications to support efficient information capture and maintenance
Liaise with staff and business partners on programs, projects, and requests
Maintain Governance & Documentation


What We’re Looking For


Extensive experience with GIS spatial databases, Esri ArcSDE Geodatabase Management
Considerable experience with Esri’s Platform, including Enterprise, ArcGIS Online, and ArcGIS Pro
Considerable experience with Feature Manipulation Engine, FME Form & Flow
Experience rationalizing customer needs, solution development in an IT environment
Demonstrated ability to foster continuous improvement and an innovation building mindset, blending automation, scripting, and emerging GIS & AI tools and technologies, to enhance operational efficiency and service delivery


Key Duties and Responsibilities

Data Management And Project Management Functions


Advance and enhance data governance as part of ITs Data Framework.
Lead, innovate, create, and support corporate data quality assessment, data modeling methodologies, data integration best practices, process models and data flows
Develop and implement spatial geodatabases, ArcSDE geodatabases, data management systems such as mobile/field applications & web mapping applications, surveys, data analytics and other strategies that optimize efficiency and quality
Integrate spatial and non‑spatial data from enterprise systems (e.g. asset management, permitting, engineering) into analytical and productivity solutions
Consult with business partners to conduct needs requirements, assess functional and system and data requirements
Lead projects using project management principles and methodologies including the development of charters, objectives, and schedules
Lead spatial and non-spatial application development activities including design, development, and implementation
Apply software development best practices (version control, testing, documentation) to GIS solutions


Data, Analytics And Reporting & Visualization Functions


Lead, innovate, create, and support corporate reporting initiatives such as visualizations through reports, mapping, and dashboard best practices including documentation of governance and process
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets against key performance indicators and industry benchmarks
Interpret data using statistical modelling to provide ongoing reports and data visualization dashboards that expands an understanding of business process, targets, compliance and opportunities for improvement
Work with business partners to develop queries for, and automation, of monthly reporting, as well as perform ad hoc queries, data extracts, and data analysis to produce actionable information around key decision making


Duties and Responsibilities Cont'd

Quality Assurance And Due Diligence Functions


Ensures quality control within spatial and non-spatial data through implementation of QA/QC procedures, development of standards, and performance monitoring as per the data governance framework
Monitors data related systems, applications, databases, and websites to ensure conformance to corporate standards and guidelines including metadata and lifecycle management
Promotes and ensures user community understands and adheres to corporate data governance for data and products
Maintains knowledge of current and emerging GIS & Data products and technologies. Provides support to corporate and divisional initiatives related to data and spatial applications including strategies, policies, standards, and procedures.


Liaison, Communication And Customer Service


Liaise with business partners, consultants, regional partnership, and vendors to ensure needs and requirements are met, and provide advice and support to user community on standards, models and applications
Participates on committees, work groups, task forces and special projects as needed
Promotes corporate data governance for spatial and non-spatial data through projects, presentations, and report preparation
Deliver user knowledge transfer and education as required


Education and Experience


University Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related discipline with an emphasis on GIS, data management and analytics
Minimum 3 years of progressive experience in enterprise platform administration, data and analytics, GIS, data management and data visualization
Experience in Data Management, GISP, PMO, Data Analytics Certifications considered an asset


Required Skills/Knowledge


Minimum of 3 years of progressively responsible and practical experience in enterprise platform administration, data management, analytics and application of visualization tools with spatial and non-spatial data
Experience working with relational and non-relational database systems including database standards, design, data models, creation methods and techniques
Extensive experience with GIS spatial databases, Esri ArcSDE Geodatabase Management
Considerable experience Managing Esri Platform: ArcGIS Enterprise, Portal, ArcGIS Server, Data Store, ArcGIS Online, application development frameworks such as Experience Builder, ArcSDE Geodatabases, license administration, security & access models
Experience working with industry data management, analytics, and visualization platforms and tools including Esri’s ArcGIS Suite. Visualization tools such as PowerBI or Tableau, Microsoft BI Stack are considered an asset
Experience in development with web-based technologies, APIs, automation suites and automation/extract-transform-load (ETL) processes; working with Safe Software's Feature Manipulation Engine FME Form and FME Flow, and Python scripting
Experience in SQL scripting to develop, implement and optimize queries, stored procedures and functions within the Microsoft Data Management platform; Querying SQL/NoSQL databases
Strong analytical, data visualization and presentation skills
Demonstrated understanding and implementation of ideation, process mapping, requirements gathering, and project planning, research and documentation
Demonstrated ability to establish and build effective working relationships with customer base and project stakeholders
Demonstrate organizational skills and ability to prioritize workload
Demonstrated ability to work independently, in small groups, and in team environments


Leadership Competencies


Builds people and culture
Cultivates open communication
Demonstrates personal leadership
Navigates and leads through complexity and change
Shapes the future


Attention Internal Candidates: All current City of Richmond Hill employees are required to apply via the ‘ View Jobs for Current Employees ’ link on the City’s Careers Page .

We thank all candidates for their interest, however, only those under consideration will be contacted.

The City of Richmond Hill is committed to inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection processes. If contacted to participate in the recruitment and selection process, please advise Human Resources if you require an accommodation.

Replacement/New Position Replacement for Existing Vacancy

Posting Type Internal and External

Posting Date 05/11/2026

Application Deadline 05/25/2026

Position Summary

We're looking for a passionate and experienced individual to join our dynamic GIS team within the City of Richmond Hill’s IT Division. You’ll play a key role in implementing the City’s GIS Strategy elements, support data driven decision making across the organization, and promote corporate data governance. You will participate in GIS Platform administration, deliver spatially enabled analytics, automation, and applications that support service optimization.

Highlights Of What You Will Do

  • Administer GIS Databases & Esri’s Platforms: Enterprise and ArcGIS Online
  • Manage Process & Product Automations
  • Design, develop, and maintain automated spatial data workflows using Python, FME, APIs, and ETL processes
  • Manage Field Tools and Web Map Applications to support efficient information capture and maintenance
  • Liaise with staff and business partners on programs, projects, and requests
  • Maintain Governance & Documentation

What We’re Looking For

  • Extensive experience with GIS spatial databases, Esri ArcSDE Geodatabase Management
  • Considerable experience with Esri’s Platform, including Enterprise, ArcGIS Online, and ArcGIS Pro
  • Considerable experience with Feature Manipulation Engine, FME Form & Flow
  • Experience rationalizing customer needs, solution development in an IT environment
  • Demonstrated ability to foster continuous improvement and an innovation building mindset, blending automation, scripting, and emerging GIS & AI tools and technologies, to enhance operational efficiency and service delivery

Key Duties and Responsibilities

Data Management And Project Management Functions

  • Advance and enhance data governance as part of ITs Data Framework.
  • Lead, innovate, create, and support corporate data quality assessment, data modeling methodologies, data integration best practices, process models and data flows
  • Develop and implement spatial geodatabases, ArcSDE geodatabases, data management systems such as mobile/field applications & web mapping applications, surveys, data analytics and other strategies that optimize efficiency and quality
  • Integrate spatial and non‑spatial data from enterprise systems (e.g. asset management, permitting, engineering) into analytical and productivity solutions
  • Consult with business partners to conduct needs requirements, assess functional and system and data requirements
  • Lead projects using project management principles and methodologies including the development of charters, objectives, and schedules
  • Lead spatial and non-spatial application development activities including design, development, and implementation
  • Apply software development best practices (version control, testing, documentation) to GIS solutions

Data, Analytics And Reporting & Visualization Functions

  • Lead, innovate, create, and support corporate reporting initiatives such as visualizations through reports, mapping, and dashboard best practices including documentation of governance and process
  • Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets against key performance indicators and industry benchmarks
  • Interpret data using statistical modelling to provide ongoing reports and data visualization dashboards that expands an understanding of business process, targets, compliance and opportunities for improvement
  • Work with business partners to develop queries for, and automation, of monthly reporting, as well as perform ad hoc queries, data extracts, and data analysis to produce actionable information around key decision making

Duties and Responsibilities Cont'd

Quality Assurance And Due Diligence Functions

  • Ensures quality control within spatial and non-spatial data through implementation of QA/QC procedures, development of standards, and performance monitoring as per the data governance framework
  • Monitors data related systems, applications, databases, and websites to ensure conformance to corporate standards and guidelines including metadata and lifecycle management
  • Promotes and ensures user community understands and adheres to corporate data governance for data and products
  • Maintains knowledge of current and emerging GIS & Data products and technologies. Provides support to corporate and divisional initiatives related to data and spatial applications including strategies, policies, standards, and procedures.

Liaison, Communication And Customer Service

  • Liaise with business partners, consultants, regional partnership, and vendors to ensure needs and requirements are met, and provide advice and support to user community on standards, models and applications
  • Participates on committees, work groups, task forces and special projects as needed
  • Promotes corporate data governance for spatial and non-spatial data through projects, presentations, and report preparation
  • Deliver user knowledge transfer and education as required

Education and Experience

  • University Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related discipline with an emphasis on GIS, data management and analytics
  • Minimum 3 years of progressive experience in enterprise platform administration, data and analytics, GIS, data management and data visualization
  • Experience in Data Management, GISP, PMO, Data Analytics Certifications considered an asset

Required Skills/Knowledge

  • Minimum of 3 years of progressively responsible and practical experience in enterprise platform administration, data management, analytics and application of visualization tools with spatial and non-spatial data
  • Experience working with relational and non-relational database systems including database standards, design, data models, creation methods and techniques
  • Extensive experience with GIS spatial databases, Esri ArcSDE Geodatabase Management
  • Considerable experience Managing Esri Platform: ArcGIS Enterprise, Portal, ArcGIS Server, Data Store, ArcGIS Online, application development frameworks such as Experience Builder, ArcSDE Geodatabases, license administration, security & access models
  • Experience working with industry data management, analytics, and visualization platforms and tools including Esri’s ArcGIS Suite. Visualization tools such as PowerBI or Tableau, Microsoft BI Stack are considered an asset
  • Experience in development with web-based technologies, APIs, automation suites and automation/extract-transform-load (ETL) processes; working with Safe Software's Feature Manipulation Engine FME Form and FME Flow, and Python scripting
  • Experience in SQL scripting to develop, implement and optimize queries, stored procedures and functions within the Microsoft Data Management platform; Querying SQL/NoSQL databases
  • Strong analytical, data visualization and presentation skills
  • Demonstrated understanding and implementation of ideation, process mapping, requirements gathering, and project planning, research and documentation
  • Demonstrated ability to establish and build effective working relationships with customer base and project stakeholders
  • Demonstrate organizational skills and ability to prioritize workload
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently, in small groups, and in team environments

Leadership Competencies

  • Builds people and culture
  • Cultivates open communication
  • Demonstrates personal leadership
  • Navigates and leads through complexity and change
  • Shapes the future

Attention Internal Candidates: All current City of Richmond Hill employees are required to apply via the ‘ View Jobs for Current Employees ’ link on the City’s Careers Page .

We thank all candidates for their interest, however, only those under consideration will be contacted.

The City of Richmond Hill is committed to inclusive, barrier-free recruitment and selection processes. If contacted to participate in the recruitment and selection process, please advise Human Resources if you require an accommodation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
THE POSITION

Our roster has an opening with your name on it

We are looking for an Analyst to join our team. You will drive insights for group-wide and commercial teams to drive actionable insights about the business. You will work alongside a group of other analysts through various individual and team assignments to continually evolve the various tools, models, and data visualizations used to drive the FanDuel Group business.

In addition to the specific responsibilities outlined above, employees may be required to perform other such duties as assigned by the Company. This ensures operational flexibility and allows the Company to meet evolving business needs.

THE GAME PLAN

Everyone on our team has a part to play


Work with other analysts to produce specific insights around customer behavior, yea over year trends, and drivers of business performance.
Create, modify and evolve models and data visualizations to better understand and explain the day-to-day performance of various commercial KPIs.
Create and maintain automated reporting processes visible to the commercial and group-wide teams.
Participate in presentations focused on using analysis to drive new business outcomes.
Field and fulfill requests for various business inquiries.



THE STATS

What we're looking for in our next teammate


Minimum of 2 years of experience in an analytics role preferred.
Bachelor’s Degree in a highly numerate major (Math, Stats, Econ, etc.) or relevant experience in data and/or business analysis.
Strong personal organizational and time management skills
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with a lot of ambiguity.
Proficiency in SQL and Excel required, experience with Python desirable.
Strong problem-solving skills, allied with track record of transforming data into actionable business insight.
Experience working in groups and leading smaller intra year projects.
Experience collaboratively working with colleagues in other countries and time zones preferred.
Interest in iGaming preferred.



About Fanduel

FanDuel Group is the premier mobile gaming company in the United States and Canada. FanDuel Group consists of a portfolio of leading brands across mobile wagering including: America’s #1 Sportsbook, FanDuel Sportsbook; its leading iGaming platform, FanDuel Casino; the industry’s unquestioned leader in horse racing and advance-deposit wagering, FanDuel Racing; and its daily fantasy sports product.

In addition, FanDuel Group operates FanDuel TV, its broadly distributed linear cable television network and FanDuel TV+, its leading direct-to-consumer OTT platform. FanDuel Group has a presence across all 50 states, Canada, and Puerto Rico.

The company is based in New York with US offices in Los Angeles, Atlanta, and Jersey City, as well as global offices in Canada and Scotland. The company’s affiliates have offices worldwide, including in Ireland, Portugal, Romania, and Australia.

FanDuel Group is a subsidiary of Flutter Entertainment, the world's largest sports betting and gaming operator with a portfolio of globally recognized brands and traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: FLUT).

Player Benefits

We treat our team right

This role may offer the following benefits: extended medical, vision, and dental coverage; life insurance; disability insurance; a retirement program with employer contributions; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law. Check out all our benefits here: FanDuel Total Rewards. *Benefits differ across country and job type.

FanDuel is an equal opportunities employer and we believe, as one of our principles states, “We are One Team!”. As such, we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or any other characteristic protected by provincial, local or federal law. We believe FanDuel is strongest and best able to compete if all employees feel valued, respected, and included.

FanDuel is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please email Benefits@fanduel.com.

The Company intends to use artificial intelligence in the process of screening, assessing, and/or selecting applicants for this role.

The applicable salary range for this position is $74,000 - $92,000 CAD, which is dependent on a variety of factors including relevant experience, location, business needs and market demand. This role may offer the following benefits: extended medical, vision, and dental coverage; life insurance; disability insurance; a retirement program with employer contributions; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law.

Title: Interchange Analyst
Description:
Summary/Objective

The primary role of the Interchange Analyst is to provide advanced technical analysis and strategic support for merchant payment data. This position focuses on identifying root causes of high processing costs, developing technical solutions for interchange optimization, and creating data-driven tools to enhance cost management for large-scale clients.




Essential Functions/ Responsibilities

Investigate payment processing data from multiple card brands and processors to identify the root cause of excessive interchange fees.
Assess complex merchant statements and understand technical payment issues to provide actionable solutions to clients.
Design, write, and enhance proprietary tools and data libraries for internal and external usage to automate cost-saving analyses.
Handle technical inquiries from internal stakeholders and external clients regarding scheme fees and optimization strategies.
Track market trends, card network programs, and industry technology developments to synthesize findings into technical recommendations.




Supervisory Responsibility

None.




Education & Experience

Education: Bachelor’s degree in CS, Finance, Economics, or a related quantitative field.
Industry: 3+ years in electronic billing/payments, including knowledge of NACHA and Card Network regulations.
Client Success: 3+ years in a technical or analytical client-facing role, with experience presenting to management and clients.

Technical Skills

Data Tools: Proficient in SQL, relational databases, and Excel.
Visualization: Experience with BI tools like Tableau.

Core Competencies

Analysis: Proven ability to analyze complex data, solve problems, and provide actionable recommendations.
Communication: Ability to translate technical terms into clear business insights for clients and internal teams.
Adaptability: Thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments.




Work Environment

This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as laptop computers, photocopiers and smartphones.




Physical Demands

This position requires extended sitting and/or standing at a computer workstation.




Position Type/Expected Hours of Work

This is a full-time position. Days and hours of work are Monday through Friday during normal business hours. Occasional evening and weekend work may be required as job duties demand.




Travel

Up to 25% travel to customer sites or internal meetings may be required




Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.




EEO Statement

Paymentus is an equal opportunity employer. We enthusiastically accept our responsibility to make employment decisions without regard to actual or perceived race, creed, color, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions), gender identity or gender expression (including transgender status), sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, religion, marital status, physical or mental disability, military service or veteran status, genetic information, protected medical condition as defined by applicable state or local law, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state, and local laws and ordinances. Our management is dedicated to ensuring the fulfillment of this policy with respect to hiring, placement, promotion, transfer, demotion, layoff, termination, recruitment advertising, pay, and other forms of compensation, training, access to facilities and programs and general treatment during employment.




Reasonable Accommodation

Paymentus recognizes and supports its obligation to endeavor to accommodate job applicants and employees with known physical or mental disabilities who are able to perform the essential functions of the position, with or without reasonable accommodation. Paymentus will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations to otherwise qualified job applicants and employees with known physical or mental disabilities, unless doing so would impose an undue hardship on the Company or pose a direct threat of substantial harm to the employee or others.




An applicant or employee who believes he or she needs a reasonable accommodation of a disability should discuss the need for possible accommodation with the Human Resources Department, or his or her direct supervisor.

Title: Data Analyst – Campaign Analytics
Description:
We are hiring a Data Analyst to join a high-performing Delivery & Personalization team supporting Canadian Banking initiatives. The team focuses on delivering personalized client experiences, campaign optimization, and data-driven marketing strategies.

The ideal candidate will have strong hands-on coding experience and experience working with large datasets, campaign execution, and analytics environments.




Responsibilities

Support marketing campaign execution, optimization, and analysis
Manage and analyze large datasets to support business objectives
Deliver accurate and optimized campaign/channel solutions
Partner with stakeholders to support revenue generation and operational effectiveness
Work with campaign execution standards, channels, and data sources
Support code migration activities across platforms
Contribute within Agile delivery environments to solve complex analytical problems




Required Skills

3+ years of experience in Data Analytics or related roles
Strong hands-on experience with Python, SAS, and SQL
Experience working with Teradata, Unix, DataLake, or Cloud environments
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to combine business understanding with technical expertise
Experience handling large datasets and campaign-related processes




Nice to Have

Banking or Financial Services experience
Exposure to marketing analytics or campaign delivery
Understanding of test & learn methodologies and statistical significance
Strong adaptability and willingness to learn new tools/processes

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Looking for a Data Analyst to support reporting, automation and dashboarding across our current on-premise Citrix Virtualization environments and soon on Windows 365 CloudPC across the Azure environment. This role focuses on data extraction, transformation, and visualization with exposure to automation and familiarity with Devops tools.

What will you do?


Build and maintain reports using PowerShell Scripting and Advanced Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
Strong SQL data extraction and reporting (joins, aggregations, group, filtering and data transformation)
Extract data using REST API from ServiceNow, Microsoft Graph API from Microsoft Intune and Entra.
Build dashboards using Tableau. Kibana dashboard
Use GitHub and Visual Studio Code for version control and development
Familiarity with developing and running Python scripts (modifying existing code, using RBC's AI tooling where helpful)
Experience with automation workflows on CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Helios, Terraform is a plus.
Create and document procedures, ServiceNow Knowledge Articles and Internal RBC Connect Web Pages related to the solutions we support


What you need to succeed?

Must Haves:


Advance experience with Visual Studio Or PowerShell Scripting
Advanced Excel experience (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
Strong SQL
Basic Python (can read/edit scripts; not expected to build complex systems)


Nice to Have:


Exposure to GitHub and Visual Studio Code
Willingness to use AI tools like GitHub Copilot
Knowledgeable with REST APIs and familiarity with Microsoft Graph API is a plus


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
A world-class training program in financial services
Flexible work/life balance options
Opportunities to do challenging work
Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
Opportunities to build close relationships with clients


#TECHPJ

Job Skills

Active Learning, Active Learning, Adaptability, Analytical Thinking, Customer Service, Dashboard Reporting, Data Extraction, Data Handling, Data Quality, Datasource, Data Transformation, Decision Making, Detail-Oriented, DevOps Tools, ElasticSearch, Excel Query, Excel Reports, GitHub, GitHub Copilot, Graph API, Group Problem Solving, IT Standards, Kibana, Lifelong Learning, Long Term Planning {+ 12 more}

Job Description

Looking for a Data Analyst to support reporting, automation and dashboarding across our current on-premise Citrix Virtualization environments and soon on Windows 365 CloudPC across the Azure environment. This role focuses on data extraction, transformation, and visualization with exposure to automation and familiarity with Devops tools.

What will you do?

  • Build and maintain reports using PowerShell Scripting and Advanced Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
  • Strong SQL data extraction and reporting (joins, aggregations, group, filtering and data transformation)
  • Extract data using REST API from ServiceNow, Microsoft Graph API from Microsoft Intune and Entra.
  • Build dashboards using Tableau. Kibana dashboard
  • Use GitHub and Visual Studio Code for version control and development
  • Familiarity with developing and running Python scripts (modifying existing code, using RBC's AI tooling where helpful)
  • Experience with automation workflows on CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Helios, Terraform is a plus.
  • Create and document procedures, ServiceNow Knowledge Articles and Internal RBC Connect Web Pages related to the solutions we support

What you need to succeed?

Must Haves:

  • Advance experience with Visual Studio Or PowerShell Scripting
  • Advanced Excel experience (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
  • Strong SQL
  • Basic Python (can read/edit scripts; not expected to build complex systems)

Nice to Have:

  • Exposure to GitHub and Visual Studio Code
  • Willingness to use AI tools like GitHub Copilot
  • Knowledgeable with REST APIs and familiarity with Microsoft Graph API is a plus

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

  • A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
  • Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
  • Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
  • Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
  • A world-class training program in financial services
  • Flexible work/life balance options
  • Opportunities to do challenging work
  • Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
  • Opportunities to build close relationships with clients

#TECHPJ

Active Learning, Active Learning, Adaptability, Analytical Thinking, Customer Service, Dashboard Reporting, Data Extraction, Data Handling, Data Quality, Datasource, Data Transformation, Decision Making, Detail-Oriented, DevOps Tools, ElasticSearch, Excel Query, Excel Reports, GitHub, GitHub Copilot, Graph API, Group Problem Solving, IT Standards, Kibana, Lifelong Learning, Long Term Planning {+ 12 more}

Title: BI Reporting Developer Analyst
Description:
Job Description:

Role Overview

We are seeking a Development Analyst to join our Business Intelligence and Reporting team. You will design, build, and optimize BI assets—dashboards, reports, data models—primarily using AWS QuickSight and SQL, turning data into actionable insights that drive business decisions. If you thrive at the intersection of data, visualization, and stakeholder collaboration, this role is for you.

Compensation & Role Details:

Expected Salary Range: The expected base salary range for this role is between $100,000 – $115,000 CAD, depending on experience and qualifications

Role Type: New Role

AI Disclosure: AI may be used during the hiring process.

Key Responsibilities


BI Solution Development:
Build interactive dashboards in AWS QuickSight (including parameters, controls, calculated fields, and narratives).
Implement data visualizations that effectively communicate business insights.
Implement row‑level security and data governance standards across BI assets.
Data Analysis & Modeling:
Write and optimize complex SQL for data extraction, transformation, aggregation, and performance tuning.
Design semantic layers and curated datasets aligned to reporting needs.
Requirements Gathering:
Collaborate with business users to understand reporting requirements.
Translate business needs into technical specifications.
Contribute to ETL specifications and unit testing for data quality and lineage.
Performance Optimization:
Ensure dashboards and queries are optimized for performance and scalability.
Monitor and troubleshoot BI solutions for accuracy and efficiency.
Documentation & Standards:
Maintain documentation for BI processes, data models, and dashboards.
Adhere to data governance and security standards.

Must‑Have Qualifications


2–5 years in BI/Reporting roles with measurable delivery of dashboards and analytics.
Strong SQL: complex joins, window functions, CTEs, aggregations, performance optimization.
Hands‑on experience with AWS QuickSight (visual types, RLS, parameters, datasets, SPICE).
Working knowledge of AWS data services (Athena, Redshift, RDS, S3, Glue/IAM basics).
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts (star/snowflake schemas, dimensional modeling).
Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
Experience using AI tools to accelerate development.


Nice‑to‑Have Skills


Experience with ETL tools (Glue jobs, dbt, or similar) and CI/CD for BI assets.
Scripting (Python) for data wrangling or automation.
Exposure to data governance (cataloging, lineage, definitions), and security (IAM/RLS).
Knowledge of performance tuning in QuickSight (SPICE capacity, query pushdown, visuals optimization).
Familiarity with visualization best practices and UX for analytics.


Key Competencies


Data Storytelling: Can translate complex data into clear visuals and narratives.
Customer Focus: Collaborates with business partners to prioritize high‑value insights.
Bias for Action: Ships iteratively, validates quickly, and improves continuously.
Quality & Accuracy: High attention to detail; strong testing discipline.
Ownership: Proactive in monitoring, documentation, and support


Business Unit:

Jonas Club

Scheduled Weekly Hours:

37.5

Number of Openings Available:

1

Worker Type:

Regular

More About Jonas Software:

Jonas Software is a leading provider of enterprise management software solutions, serving a wide range of vertical markets including hospitality, healthcare, construction, education, personal care, fitness, leisure, moving and legal services, to name a few. Within these markets, Jonas is comprised of over 65 distinct brands, each a respected leader in its domain.

Jonas’ vision is to be the branded global leader across these verticals and to be recognized by customers and industry stakeholders as the trusted provider of “Software for Life.” We are committed to technology, product innovation, quality, and exceptional customer service.

Jonas Software supports over 60,000 customers in more than 30 countries. We employ over 6,000 skilled professionals, including industry experts and technology specialists. Across our broader network, we support a global workforce of more than 30,000 employees.

Headquartered in Canada, Jonas Software has a global footprint with offices around the world. We’re a 100% owned subsidiary of Constellation Software Inc., based in Toronto, publicly listed on the TSX (CSU.TO), and a member of the S&P/TSX 60 Index.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
Join our Pricing team as a Pricing Analyst, where you’ll play a key role in bringing accurate pricing to life for our online‑only assortment. In this role, you’ll own pricing and freight calculations, perform quality checks, and help ensure pricing changes are executed correctly across systems. You’ll collaborate closely with Pricing, Merchandising, and Market Intelligence teams—making this a great opportunity for someone who’s detail‑oriented, analytical, and excited to work at the intersection of pricing, data, and operations.

What You'll Do


Calculate, validate, and quality‑check regular pricing for online‑only SKUs, ensuring inputs align to pricing rules, business logic, and governance standards.
Work closely with Pricing Specialists to ensure approved price changes are accurately executed in downstream systems in a timely manner.
Own freight calculation activities for online‑only SKUs, including the calculation and maintenance of freight % assumptions used in pricing.
Support the development and enhancement of pricing tools, templates, and workflows (e.g., calculators, KNIME‑based processes, or Power BI outputs) that improve accuracy, efficiency, and scalability over time.
Contribute to process improvements by identifying gaps, inefficiencies, or risks in current pricing and execution workflows and partnering with Pricing Strategy to evolve the future state.
Maintain and update SOPs, process documentation, and knowledge‑transfer materials to support consistency, continuity, and scalability as online‑only assortment grows.
Support project and change initiatives related to 2P pricing operations, including testing, data validation, and coordination with cross‑functional partners as processes and systems evolve.
Partner with Merchandising and Market Intelligence to document and validate competitive data inputs.
Own delivery against defined project timelines and milestones, ensuring accuracy and timely execution of pricing-related deliverables.


What You'll Bring


Attention to Detail: Strong focus on accuracy, data quality, and validation in high‑volume operational work.
Analytical Thinking: Comfortable working with pricing logic, calculations, and structured problem solving.
Process Orientation: Interest in improving, documenting, and standardizing repeatable pricing workflows.
Collaboration: Ability to work cross‑functionally with Pricing Specialists, Pricing Strategy, Merchandising, and various cross-functional teams.
Accountability: Takes ownership of routine but critical workstreams and manages day‑to‑day priorities independently.
Learning Mindset: Curious and motivated to build skills in tools such as KNIME, Power BI, and pricing platforms.


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, or related field.
1-3 years of experience in pricing, analytics, or operations roles.
Experience working with data sets, calculations, and QC processes.
Familiarity with pricing tools, automation tools, or BI platforms (e.g., PowerBI) is an asset.
Experience supporting SOP creation, data testing, or operational process improvement is preferred.


We’re always looking for great talent! In addition to competitive pay, we offer:


Career growth opportunities and product discounts


Broadband Salary Range: $23.00/hr – $37.00/hr.

Our typical hiring range is between $23.00/hr and $30.00/hr. Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements. We're committed to attracting top talent. For critical roles, the compensation offering will be reviewed to ensure alignment with market rate and conditions and the unique value you bring to the role.

We may use artificial intelligence tools as part of our recruitment process to assist in the initial screening of resumes. All hiring decisions, including candidate evaluation, selection, and disposition, are made by human recruiters.

About Us

Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited (“CTC”) is one of Canada’s most admired and trusted companies. With more than 90 Owned Brands, 1,700 retail locations, financial services, exemplary e-commerce capabilities, and exciting market-leading merchandising strategies. We dream big and work as one to innovate with purpose for our customers at every level of our business, investing in new technologies and products, and doubling down on top talent to drive the company forward. We offer competitive salaries and wages to CTC employees, as well as store discounts, supported learning through our Triangle Learning Academy, Canadian Tire Profit Sharing, and retirement and savings programs for eligible employees. As part of our enhanced flex benefits program, we offer mental health benefits in the amount of $5,000 per year for benefits-eligible employees and their families, including total well-being, and mental health tools and resources for all employees. Join us in helping to make life in Canada better through living and working our Core Values: we are innovators and entrepreneurs at our core, outcomes drive us, inclusion is a must, we are stronger together and we take personal responsibility. It is an especially exciting time to join CTC and its family of companies where career opportunities are wide-ranging! Join us, where there's a place for you here.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

We are committed to fostering an environment where belonging thrives, and diversity, inclusion and equity are infused into everything we do. We believe in building an organizational culture where people are consistently treated with dignity while respecting individual religion, nationality, gender, race, age, perceived ability, spoken language, sexual orientation, and identification. We are united in our purpose of being here to help make life in Canada better.

Accommodations

We stand firm in our Core Value that inclusion is a must. We welcome and encourage candidates from equity-seeking groups such as people who identify as racialized, Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, women, people with disabilities, and beyond. Should you require any accommodation in applying for this role, or throughout the interview process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to help meet your needs.

Title: (PERMANENT FULL TIME) BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE DEVELOPER
Company: Ontario Shores Centre for Mental Health Sciences
Description:
Job Details

Description

As part of the Data & Analytics team, the Business Intelligence (BI) Developer will report directly to the Director, Data, Analytics & Quality, with day-to-day support and coaching from the Senior BI Developer & Technical Lead. The BI Developer will support, in collaboration with other team members, designing, modeling, development, maintenance and enhancement of the Ontario Shores’ data environments and tools/solutions. The role will also support reporting, analytics as well as business intelligence/decision support solutions to meet the information needs and the strategic goals of the organization. The BI Developer will apply best practices in data analytics, requirements gathering, data modelling and report building. There will also be work involving APIs, JSON, Blazor Web App Development (C# and HTML), and Azure Cloud tools.

Key Duties And Responsibilities

Database/Data Model Design & Development:


Documents business requirements in the form of specifications (functional and technical specifications), validates accuracy and obtains necessary input and approval.
Supports development and maintenance of data models and data dictionaries, data maps and other artifacts across the organization, including the conceptual and physical models, as well as metadata repository.
Contributes to evaluation of current state and future state requirements and supports development of proposals to address identified gaps.
Supports activities to ensure the integrity and performance of the Data Warehouse/Business Intelligence environment at Ontario Shores, including investigation and issue resolution as required.


Report Design & Development


Contributes to the development of reporting solutions and helps to ensures that report implementation produces the required output as defined by the requirements.
Generates reports using reporting tools such as Power BI Report Builder, Power BI Desktop, and Excel spreadsheets; schedules and deploys reports on Microsoft Fabric
Ensures reports are optimized for efficiency and performance in production environment.
Assists with documentation of report library and builds ETL mechanisms using tools such as Azure Logic Apps and Azure Data Factory.


Technical Leadership and Guidance


Lead collaboration with IT at Ontario Shores and within the MH-HIS Cluster as applicable to help ensure internal control & integrity of systems.
Support team members in knowledge and skill development as applicable, including students
Conduct research into data trends, issues, standards, and products as required.
Contributes ideas towards best practice improvements, process improvement, and innovative solutions to ensure the stability and reliability of the Data Warehouse/Business Intelligence environment at Ontario Shores and the shared cloud-based data & analytics environment.
Perform system and platform performance monitoring and tuning, and SQL code review.
Support and follow information governance and data standardization procedures established by the organization.
Suggest appropriate content and design details where appropriate,
Participate and support activities as part of various Data Management Working Groups.


Other Duties


Application development using C# via console applications to support utility programming
Maintains strict confidentiality in handling of any sensitive data.
On-call availability after hours will be required to support business critical products and/or services.
Performs other related duties as assigned or requested.


Education

QUALIFICATIONS REQUIRED AND PREFERRED


Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or Information Technology, or equivalent


Qualifications


2-3 years of work experience with SQL server, Business Intelligence, reporting and data modelling


Knowledge & Skills


Working knowledge of relational databases, data model development, data warehousing, and business intelligence.
Working knowledge in working with RESTful APIs and non-relational data formats such as JSON/XML.
Competency in database design, relational integrity constraints, data marts, and normalization.
Experience in generating custom reports using SQL Server and Microsoft reporting tools such as SSMS, Power BI desktop, including advanced functions, scheduling and publishing reports on Microsoft Fabric
Demonstrated experience in creating medium to large-scale enterprise logical data models using normalization, star and snowflake schema techniques.
Advanced skills in reviewing SQL code and making recommendations for optimization.
Procedural skills to help design, debug, implement, and maintain stored procedures, views, triggers and user-defined functions.
Demonstrated experience in responding to and troubleshooting issues related to data quality and performance, with the ability to resolve them in a timely manner.
Demonstrated ability to translate complex business requirement into technical solutions
Demonstrated experience in building complex data analytics reporting
Hands on experience with Object-Oriented Programming language, preferably C#
The ability to foster collaborative and effective working relationships with stakeholders that promote cooperative goals and contribute to an atmosphere of trust and mutual respect.
Excellent communication, interpersonal, organizational, change management and relationship management skills.
Proven abilities to take initiative and to be innovative and collaborative.
Strong problem-solving skills to investigate and resolve any technical/performance issues that occur.
Experience in working on multiple projects at the same time.
Demonstrated team player with a proven track record for meeting deadlines.
Ability to work with minimal supervision in an agile environment.
Models and promotes core ethical practices, organizational values, and reflects an optimistic and positive attitude.
Project management skills


The Following Will Be Considered As Preferred


Working knowledge of healthcare and/or MEDITECH HIS environment.
Experience with cloud-based BI, data virtualization including Azure platform and Microsoft Fabric.
Good understanding of, and experience with, code repositories such as GitHub and DevOps for managing code.
Good understanding of, and experience with, the software development lifecycle and software project management approaches.
Good understanding of N-Tier Web Architecture, Repository design pattern, and experience with HTML, CSS and Java Script in website development with various Web Frameworks.
Knowledge of the basics of Machine Learning and ability to write/read Python/R code.

  • Performs other related duties as assigned or requested.

Education

QUALIFICATIONS REQUIRED AND PREFERRED

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or Information Technology, or equivalent

Qualifications

  • 2-3 years of work experience with SQL server, Business Intelligence, reporting and data modelling

Knowledge & Skills

  • Working knowledge of relational databases, data model development, data warehousing, and business intelligence.
  • Working knowledge in working with RESTful APIs and non-relational data formats such as JSON/XML.
  • Competency in database design, relational integrity constraints, data marts, and normalization.
  • Experience in generating custom reports using SQL Server and Microsoft reporting tools such as SSMS, Power BI desktop, including advanced functions, scheduling and publishing reports on Microsoft Fabric
  • Demonstrated experience in creating medium to large-scale enterprise logical data models using normalization, star and snowflake schema techniques.
  • Advanced skills in reviewing SQL code and making recommendations for optimization.
  • Procedural skills to help design, debug, implement, and maintain stored procedures, views, triggers and user-defined functions.
  • Demonstrated experience in responding to and troubleshooting issues related to data quality and performance, with the ability to resolve them in a timely manner.
  • Demonstrated ability to translate complex business requirement into technical solutions
  • Demonstrated experience in building complex data analytics reporting
  • Hands on experience with Object-Oriented Programming language, preferably C#
  • The ability to foster collaborative and effective working relationships with stakeholders that promote cooperative goals and contribute to an atmosphere of trust and mutual respect.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal, organizational, change management and relationship management skills.
  • Proven abilities to take initiative and to be innovative and collaborative.
  • Strong problem-solving skills to investigate and resolve any technical/performance issues that occur.
  • Experience in working on multiple projects at the same time.
  • Demonstrated team player with a proven track record for meeting deadlines.
  • Ability to work with minimal supervision in an agile environment.
  • Models and promotes core ethical practices, organizational values, and reflects an optimistic and positive attitude.
  • Project management skills

The Following Will Be Considered As Preferred

  • Working knowledge of healthcare and/or MEDITECH HIS environment.
  • Experience with cloud-based BI, data virtualization including Azure platform and Microsoft Fabric.
  • Good understanding of, and experience with, code repositories such as GitHub and DevOps for managing code.
  • Good understanding of, and experience with, the software development lifecycle and software project management approaches.
  • Good understanding of N-Tier Web Architecture, Repository design pattern, and experience with HTML, CSS and Java Script in website development with various Web Frameworks.
  • Knowledge of the basics of Machine Learning and ability to write/read Python/R code.

This is a new vacancy

Title: Specialist Cyber Analytics
Description:
Job ID: AR 637

Job Title: Specialist Cyber Analytics

Division: Toronto Cyber Security

Reports To: Manager Strategic Transformation

Location: Toronto, Ontario

Employment Type: Permanent Full-Time, Monday to Friday, 35 hours work week

Salary: $129,000.00




Consider your new role with Aliant Resources and our municipal government client, The City of Toronto.




About Us

Aliant Resources is a dedicated provider of IT staffing services, committed to promoting diversity, equity, inclusion, belonging, anti-racism, and accessibility in all facets of our operations.




Job Summary

To design, develop, and maintain cloud-based data pipelines, backend services, and analytics workflows that support cyber security reporting, risk insights, and executive and operational decision-making, using hands-on coding, automation, and modern development practices.




To build, enhance, and operationally support scalable data and analytics platforms, working collaboratively across teams to ensure data quality, pipeline resiliency, CI/CD-driven delivery, and reduced long-term reliance on external development and implementation partners.




To configure, extend, and assume technical ownership of cyber risk and governance platforms (e.g., GRC/CSRM-type tools) following core implementation, providing ongoing development, workflow automation, integrations, and operational support while contributing to backlog management and delivery execution.




Major Responsibilities

Support the design, development, and maintenance of cloud-hosted data pipelines for cyber dashboards, metrics, and risk reporting under senior guidance.
Assist with implementing and updating data ingestion and integration patterns as data sources and reporting needs evolve.
Contribute to the reliability, performance, and scalability of data pipelines through monitoring, testing, and incremental improvements.
Support automated data ingestion, transformation, validation, and reconciliation workflows to ensure consistent data processing.
Assist with troubleshooting pipeline failures, data quality issues, and operational incidents, escalating complex issues as required.
Support secure file-based and API-driven data ingestion processes, acting as a secondary technical resource for integrations and file operations.
Perform data cleansing, transformation, and validation to support accurate, auditable, and fit-for-purpose reporting outputs.
Apply coding and development best practices (version control, testing, documentation) when contributing to scripts, workflows, and automation.
Assist with maintaining and supporting production dashboards, including updates, performance tuning, publishing, and operational support.
Support the configuration and day-to-day operation of analytics, governance, and workflow platforms (e.g., GRC/CSRM tools), including access controls and compliance requirements.




Key Qualifications

Postsecondary degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Analytics, or a related discipline, or an equivalent combination of education, training, and practical experience.
Demonstrated experience applying data analytics and analytical techniques to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies across complex datasets, including operational, security, or risk-related data.
Hands-on experience designing, developing, and supporting data and analytics solutions in modern computing environments, with a strong preference for AWS-based platforms. This includes practical experience with AWS services such as S3, Glue, Lambda, Redshift, Step Functions, and Workspaces, and the design of scalable ETL pipelines and orchestration workflows.
Experience with comparable Azure or Google Cloud services (e.g., Azure Data Factory, Synapse, Data Lake, Functions; GCP BigQuery, Dataflow, Cloud Storage) is considered an asset.
Strong knowledge of data management principles, including data modeling, querying, transformation, and validation, to enable reliable, auditable, and fit-for-purpose reporting and analysis.
Proven experience extracting, cleaning, transforming, and analyzing data from multiple sources, and collaborating with data owners or subject matter experts to understand data context, quality considerations, and limitations.
Experience designing, building, and maintaining reports, dashboards, and analytical outputs that support strategic, operational, and executive decision-making.
Working knowledge of database query languages and analytics or development tools used for data processing, automation, and workflow enablement, including version control systems (e.g., Git) and modern development practices.
Experience applying analytics, data engineering, or reporting capabilities within security-aligned, risk-focused, regulatory, or governance contexts is considered an asset.
Broad understanding of enterprise information technology environments, with the ability to quickly learn and adapt to new platforms, tools, and architectures.
Exposure to or experience working in security-conscious environments where data integrity, access controls, privacy, and responsible handling of sensitive information are critical.
Relevant technical or security-focused certifications—particularly AWS certifications (AWS Certified Data Engineer), with Azure or GCP certifications as nice-to-have—are considered an asset.




Skills

Demonstrated ability to work effectively within transformative, technology-driven programs, adapting to evolving requirements and platforms.
Proven ability to communicate and collaborate efficiently with diverse stakeholders, including technical teams, business partners, leadership, and external vendors, to achieve shared outcomes.
Strong capability to influence and drive business objectives through effective stakeholder engagement and sound technical judgment.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the confidence to communicate complex technical concepts clearly across all organizational levels.
Advanced problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze, diagnose, and resolve complex or non-routine technical and operational challenges.
Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and commitment to producing accurate, reliable, and auditable outputs.
Highly self-motivated and proactive, able to work independently while contributing effectively as part of a collaborative team.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment, managing multiple initiatives and competing priorities while meeting strict deadlines.
Strong analytical, prioritization, and multitasking skills, with the ability to assess trade-offs and make informed decisions.
Ability to perform effectively under pressure, maintaining professionalism, focus, and sound judgment.
Strong sense of ownership and accountability, with a demonstrated willingness to go beyond assigned tasks to ensure successful delivery and operational continuity.
Demonstrated ability to think critically and respond decisively, particularly in security-relevant or time-sensitive situations, where sound judgment and rapid response are essential.




Diversity Statement

At Aliant Resources, we are committed to building a workforce that reflects the communities we serve and to promoting a diverse, anti-racist, inclusive, accessible, merit-based, respectful and equitable workplace.




Accessibility Statement

Aliant Resources is dedicated to making our recruitment process accessible to everyone with or without a disability. We adhere to workplace accessibility standards to ensure that individuals with disabilities can fully participate in employment opportunities. We understand the importance of identifying and removing barriers and we strive to provide an inclusive experience for all candidates.




Workplace Accommodations

We offer workplace accommodations throughout the recruitment process and all aspects of employment consistent with the requirements of the AODA.




If you require workplace accommodations due to a disability, injury, illness, or any other condition that may affect your ability to apply online or perform essential job functions, please reach out to us at accommodations@aliantresources.com. Your request will be answered and we will collaborate with you to provide appropriate workplace accommodations as appropriate.




How to Apply

You must apply online here on LinkedIn.
Your resume should not exceed four (4) pages, in Microsoft Word or PDF format only.




Note for all Applicants

We thank all applicants for their interest but advise that only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.
Qualified applicants may be considered for other comparable positions.
Please prepare your application in accordance with the qualifications posted in this job advertisement.
Subject to a police check, background check, psychological assessment and/or any other checks on a regular basis as the Toronto Cyber Security handles highly sensitive and confidential information.

Title: Data & Reporting Analyst
Description:
💼 Are you someone who enjoys transforming raw data into meaningful business insights while helping teams make smarter, data-driven decisions?




We're hiring a Data & Reporting Analyst to join a well-established, collaborative organization where data plays a critical role in supporting business operations, reporting, and decision-making across multiple departments.




This is an opportunity for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and enjoys working across reporting, business intelligence, data warehousing, and analytics. You'll work closely with business users to understand reporting needs, build scalable reporting solutions, and help improve how data is leveraged across the organization.




💻 Who You Are




A detail-oriented data professional who enjoys solving business problems through data and analytics
Someone who can confidently work with both technical teams and business stakeholders
Experienced in building reports, dashboards, and BI solutions within Microsoft-based environments
A strong analytical thinker with a passion for data accuracy and continuous improvement
Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Someone who enjoys both independent work and cross-functional collaboration
A curious and adaptable individual who enjoys learning new technologies and tools




📍 Work Type




Location: North York, ON (Fully Onsite - 5 days a week in office)
Vacancy Type: This role reflects an existing vacancy




🎯 What You'll Do




Meet with business users to gather reporting and data requirements
Build reports and dashboards using SSRS, SQL Server, and Excel
Develop and maintain cubes and dimensional data models using SSAS and MDX
Support and enhance SQL-based data warehouse environments
Assist end users with data analysis, interpretation, and reporting needs
Troubleshoot reporting, data, and integration issues as they arise
Support testing activities including unit testing, integration testing, and user acceptance testing
Create and maintain documentation related to reporting and data processes
Research and resolve technical issues using available resources and tools
Contribute to ongoing improvements across reporting, analytics, and BI processes
Stay current with evolving technologies, tools, and best practices within data and analytics




⭐ What We're Looking For




4+ years of experience in data analysis, reporting, business intelligence, or related roles
Strong experience working within Microsoft SQL Server environments (SQL 2017+)
Hands-on experience with SSRS, SSAS, and SSIS
Advanced T-SQL experience including DDL and DML queries
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and BI solution development
Experience building dashboards and reports for business users
Knowledge of dimensional modeling and relational database design
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills including pivot tables, formulas, and VBA
Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving capabilities
Excellent communication skills with the ability to work directly with end users and stakeholders
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment




Nice to have:




Experience with Power BI or Jet Reports
Professional certifications related to Microsoft, BI, or Data Analytics are considered an asset




🔥 What Makes This Role Exciting




Impact: Your work will directly support business decision-making across multiple departments
Variety: Balance reporting, analytics, troubleshooting, and business collaboration in one role
Collaboration: Work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders across the organization
Stability: Join a well-established organization with long-term growth and stability




Benefits




Base Salary: $70,000 - $80,000
Paid Time Off: Competitive vacation and personal days
Health & Dental Benefits: Comprehensive coverage to support your overall well-being




📩 Ready to Turn Data into Meaningful Insights? Apply Now!




At STACK IT Recruitment, we connect top talent with standout opportunities. If you meet at least 70% of the qualifications, we encourage you to apply — we'd love to chat!




Know someone perfect for this role? Share it with your network — you might just help someone take the next step in their career.

STACK IT uses AI-enhanced tools to support initial candidate screening and interview note analysis. All assessments and hiring decisions remain human-led.
