Title: Data Analyst
Description:
You are as unique as your background, experience and point of view. Here, you’ll be encouraged, empowered and challenged to be your best self. You'll work with dynamic colleagues - experts in their fields - who are eager to share their knowledge with you. Your leaders will inspire and help you reach your potential and soar to new heights. Every day, you'll have new and exciting opportunities to make life brighter for our Clients - who are at the heart of everything we do. Discover how you can make a difference in the lives of individuals, families and communities around the world.

Job Description:

Do you thrive at the intersection of business and technology? Do you have the communication skills to bridge technical and non-technical stakeholders? If so, consider a career in the Health Analytics team at Sun Life.

This role is pivotal in connecting what our business needs with what our technology can deliver. You'll work closely with business leaders to understand their goals, then partner with IT teams to bring those solutions to life. Your ability to simplify complexity and eliminate unnecessary steps will directly empower employees across the organization—giving them more time to focus on what truly matters. Beyond the immediate impact, this role offers opportunities to deepen your expertise in emerging technologies, positioning you for continued growth and advancement in an increasingly digital landscape.

Reporting to Senior Data Engineer, the Data Analyst is responsible for developing automation solutions and supporting data engineering best practices of our Health Analytics department.

What will you do?


Transform ad-hoc data requests to automated, self serve solutions.
Reverse engineer legacy reports, collaborate with business to improve them and automate by data pipelines in the cloud.
Support data engineering best practices.
Identify and participate in initiatives for process automation and optimization
Develop innovative solutions for business challenges using critical thinking and problem-solving skills
Own, create, and maintain documentation to support knowledge sharing and ensure appropriate backup coverage


What do you need to succeed?


Technical skills:
Strong proficiency in SQL including complex joins, window functions.
Experience with Python for tooling, and workflow development.
Practical experience with AWS (lambda functions, glue jobs, step functions, CloudWatch etc.) or equivalent cloud platforms.
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts, ETL patterns.
Proficiency with Git and version control workflows.
Detail-oriented: able to follow process notes, carefully preserve data integrity and ensure quality output with minimal to no errors/issues
Communication skills: excellent collaboration with strong written and verbal communication skills; ability to document processes
Takes initiative: resourceful at problem solving and continually improving job processes and procedures. Ability to work independently, as well as part of a team
Adaptability: ability to adapt to changing priorities and manage time effectively to handle multiple tasks concurrently


What will be nice to have:


Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines.
Familiarity with Spark data frames, Spark SQL, PySpark
Exposure to BI tools (e.g., Tableau, QuickSight, Power BI).
Experience using GenAI tools to improve productivity in SQL development, data exploration, and documentation


What’s in it for you?


We’re proud to be a Best Workplace for Hybrid Work by Great Place to Work® Canada
Be part of our continuous improvement journey in developing the next greatest digital enterprise experience that is united by our Purpose: to help Clients and Employees achieve lifetime financial security and live healthier lives.
Flexible Benefits from the day you join to meet the needs of you and your family.
Pension, stock and savings programs to help build and enhance your future financial security.


The Base Pay range is for the primary location for which the job is posted. It may vary depending on the work location of the successful candidate or other factors. In addition to Base Pay, eligible Sun Life employees participate in various incentive plans, payment under which is discretionary and subject to individual and company performance. Certain sales focused roles have sales incentive plans based on individual or group sales results.

Diversity and inclusion have always been at the core of our values at Sun Life. A diverse workforce with wide perspectives and creative ideas benefits our Clients, the communities where we operate and all of us as colleagues. We welcome applications from qualified individuals from all backgrounds.

Persons with disabilities who need accommodation in the application process, or those needing job postings in an alternative format, may e-mail a request to thebrightside@sunlife.com.

We are proud to be a hybrid organization that offers our employees the choice and flexibility to work from both the office and virtually based on the needs of the business, our Clients and you! Several work options are available and can be discussed throughout the selection process depending on the role requirements and individual needs.

We may use artificial intelligence to support candidate sourcing, screening, interview scheduling.

We thank all applicants for showing an interest in this position. Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
THE POSITION

Our roster has an opening with your name on it

We are looking for an Analyst to join our team. You will drive insights for group-wide and commercial teams to drive actionable insights about the business. You will work alongside a group of other analysts through various individual and team assignments to continually evolve the various tools, models, and data visualizations used to drive the FanDuel Group business.

In addition to the specific responsibilities outlined above, employees may be required to perform other such duties as assigned by the Company. This ensures operational flexibility and allows the Company to meet evolving business needs.

THE GAME PLAN

Everyone on our team has a part to play


Work with other analysts to produce specific insights around customer behavior, yea over year trends, and drivers of business performance.
Create, modify and evolve models and data visualizations to better understand and explain the day-to-day performance of various commercial KPIs.
Create and maintain automated reporting processes visible to the commercial and group-wide teams.
Participate in presentations focused on using analysis to drive new business outcomes.
Field and fulfill requests for various business inquiries.



THE STATS

What we're looking for in our next teammate


Minimum of 2 years of experience in an analytics role preferred.
Bachelor’s Degree in a highly numerate major (Math, Stats, Econ, etc.) or relevant experience in data and/or business analysis.
Strong personal organizational and time management skills
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with a lot of ambiguity.
Proficiency in SQL and Excel required, experience with Python desirable.
Strong problem-solving skills, allied with track record of transforming data into actionable business insight.
Experience working in groups and leading smaller intra year projects.
Experience collaboratively working with colleagues in other countries and time zones preferred.
Interest in iGaming preferred.



About Fanduel

FanDuel Group is the premier mobile gaming company in the United States and Canada. FanDuel Group consists of a portfolio of leading brands across mobile wagering including: America’s #1 Sportsbook, FanDuel Sportsbook; its leading iGaming platform, FanDuel Casino; the industry’s unquestioned leader in horse racing and advance-deposit wagering, FanDuel Racing; and its daily fantasy sports product.

In addition, FanDuel Group operates FanDuel TV, its broadly distributed linear cable television network and FanDuel TV+, its leading direct-to-consumer OTT platform. FanDuel Group has a presence across all 50 states, Canada, and Puerto Rico.

The company is based in New York with US offices in Los Angeles, Atlanta, and Jersey City, as well as global offices in Canada and Scotland. The company’s affiliates have offices worldwide, including in Ireland, Portugal, Romania, and Australia.

FanDuel Group is a subsidiary of Flutter Entertainment, the world's largest sports betting and gaming operator with a portfolio of globally recognized brands and traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: FLUT).

Player Benefits

We treat our team right

This role may offer the following benefits: extended medical, vision, and dental coverage; life insurance; disability insurance; a retirement program with employer contributions; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law. Check out all our benefits here: FanDuel Total Rewards. *Benefits differ across country and job type.

THE POSITION

We are looking for an Analyst to join our team. You will drive insights for group-wide and commercial teams to drive actionable insights about the business. You will work alongside a group of other analysts through various individual and team assignments to continually evolve the various tools, models, and data visualizations used to drive the FanDuel Group business.

In addition to the specific responsibilities outlined above, employees may be required to perform other such duties as assigned by the Company. This ensures operational flexibility and allows the Company to meet evolving business needs.

THE GAME PLAN

Everyone on our team has a part to play

  • Work with other analysts to produce specific insights around customer behavior, yea over year trends, and drivers of business performance.
  • Create, modify and evolve models and data visualizations to better understand and explain the day-to-day performance of various commercial KPIs.
  • Create and maintain automated reporting processes visible to the commercial and group-wide teams.
  • Participate in presentations focused on using analysis to drive new business outcomes.
  • Field and fulfill requests for various business inquiries.


THE STATS

What we're looking for in our next teammate

  • Minimum of 2 years of experience in an analytics role preferred.
  • Bachelor’s Degree in a highly numerate major (Math, Stats, Econ, etc.) or relevant experience in data and/or business analysis.
  • Strong personal organizational and time management skills
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with a lot of ambiguity.
  • Proficiency in SQL and Excel required, experience with Python desirable.
  • Strong problem-solving skills, allied with track record of transforming data into actionable business insight.
  • Experience working in groups and leading smaller intra year projects.
  • Experience collaboratively working with colleagues in other countries and time zones preferred.
  • Interest in iGaming preferred.


About Fanduel

FanDuel Group is the premier mobile gaming company in the United States and Canada. FanDuel Group consists of a portfolio of leading brands across mobile wagering including: America’s #1 Sportsbook, FanDuel Sportsbook; its leading iGaming platform, FanDuel Casino; the industry’s unquestioned leader in horse racing and advance-deposit wagering, FanDuel Racing; and its daily fantasy sports product.

In addition, FanDuel Group operates FanDuel TV, its broadly distributed linear cable television network and FanDuel TV+, its leading direct-to-consumer OTT platform. FanDuel Group has a presence across all 50 states, Canada, and Puerto Rico.

The company is based in New York with US offices in Los Angeles, Atlanta, and Jersey City, as well as global offices in Canada and Scotland. The company’s affiliates have offices worldwide, including in Ireland, Portugal, Romania, and Australia.

FanDuel Group is a subsidiary of Flutter Entertainment, the world's largest sports betting and gaming operator with a portfolio of globally recognized brands and traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: FLUT).

Player Benefits

We treat our team right

This role may offer the following benefits: extended medical, vision, and dental coverage; life insurance; disability insurance; a retirement program with employer contributions; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law. Check out all our benefits here: FanDuel Total Rewards. *Benefits differ across country and job type.

FanDuel is an equal opportunities employer and we believe, as one of our principles states, “We are One Team!”. As such, we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or any other characteristic protected by provincial, local or federal law. We believe FanDuel is strongest and best able to compete if all employees feel valued, respected, and included.

FanDuel is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please email Benefits@fanduel.com.

The Company intends to use artificial intelligence in the process of screening, assessing, and/or selecting applicants for this role.

The applicable salary range for this position is $74,000 - $92,000 CAD, which is dependent on a variety of factors including relevant experience, location, business needs and market demand. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes 12 holidays and two (2) floating holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with applicable Canadian law.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Established in 1973, Roots is a proudly Canadian lifestyle brand that celebrates authenticity, quality, and community. Inspired by the great outdoors, we create timeless, sustainable apparel and leather goods designed for comfort and everyday adventure. At Roots, we believe in fostering a culture of inclusivity, creativity, and teamwork – where every team member plays a role in shaping our brand’s legacy. If you bring passion to everything you do, lead with integrity, and believe in the power of collaboration to drive growth, we’d love to have you on our journey!

Nature & Scope → Your Role at Roots

As an Analytics Engineer, you'll sit at the intersection of data engineering and analytics, turning raw retail data into trusted, scalable assets and the insights that bring them to life. As a collaborative member of our data team, you'll contribute to the data models that power solutions across the Roots business and help build the data products, semantic layers, and self-service tools that empower every team at Roots to access trusted data independently. Our data team is small and collaborative, meaning you'll wear multiple hats and have broad exposure across the business.

Key Responsibilities → How You’ll Make An Impact


Support end-to-end data transformation, converting raw omnichannel source data (POS, e-commerce, inventory, CRM, and marketing) into clean, structured layers within Snowflake.
Contribute to the design and build of components of the Snowflake data mart, applying dimensional modelling best practices including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions, to help create reliable, performant data products that serve ad hoc analysis, BI dashboards, and AI data solutions.
Support Power BI report development end-to-end, from semantic model design to polished, publish-ready reports, giving commercial, merchandising, marketing, and finance teams self-service access to trusted data without analyst dependency.
Perform ad hoc analysis using SQL and Python directly in Snowflake to help answer pressing business questions, uncover trends, and deliver timely insights that inform planning, buying, and operational decisions.
Assist in maintaining data quality standards, including testing frameworks, pipeline alerting, and documentation, to help ensure the data mart is trustworthy and well-understood across the business.
Support the development and maintenance of Gen AI data solutions, including natural language interfaces enabling any business user to query the data mart in plain English.
Partner cross-functionally with merchandising, planning, marketing, and commercial teams to understand analytical needs and help translate them into scalable data products.
Participate in the continuous improvement of our data platform, contributing ideas around new tools, patterns, and AI capabilities that keep Roots ahead of the curve.


Qualifications & Experience → The Skills You Bring


3+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, business intelligence, or an equivalent data analytics role.
Strong SQL proficiency and hands-on experience with Snowflake or a comparable cloud data warehouse.
Hands-on experience with ELT/ETL platforms, with Matillion or a similar cloud-native transformation tool preferred.
Proficiency building data models and dashboards with Power BI.
Solid understanding of dimensional modelling methodologies including star schemas and slowly changing dimensions.
Experience with version control using Git.
Familiarity with data quality practices including testing, documentation, and pipeline monitoring.
Exposure to Gen AI solutions such as Snowflake Cortex or Microsoft Fabric Copilot is preferred.
Retail or apparel industry experience across omnichannel environments is an asset.
Python proficiency for data transformation, ad hoc analysis, or automation is a plus.


At Roots we appreciate that skills and expertise are cultivated through a range of experiences. We are committed to reflecting Canada's diverse landscape in our products, team, and workplace culture. We value your unique perspective and encourage you to apply, even if you don't meet every listed requirement. Accommodations are available for applicants throughout the recruitment process.

Please note: Roots uses technology-assisted tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to support parts of the recruitment process. These tools may be used to help review applications, assess qualifications, and support interview documentation. All hiring decisions are reviewed and made by our Talent team and key leaders involved in the recruitment process.

The posted salary range is intended to reflect the competitive market value of this role and is provided to support transparency in our hiring process. Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to internal equity, relevant skills, demonstrated knowledge, experience, and overall fit for the position.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Looking for a Data Analyst to support reporting, automation and dashboarding across our current on-premise Citrix Virtualization environments and soon on Windows 365 CloudPC across the Azure environment. This role focuses on data extraction, transformation, and visualization with exposure to automation and familiarity with Devops tools.

What will you do?


Build and maintain reports using PowerShell Scripting and Advanced Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
Strong SQL data extraction and reporting (joins, aggregations, group, filtering and data transformation)
Extract data using REST API from ServiceNow, Microsoft Graph API from Microsoft Intune and Entra.
Build dashboards using Tableau. Kibana dashboard
Use GitHub and Visual Studio Code for version control and development
Familiarity with developing and running Python scripts (modifying existing code, using RBC's AI tooling where helpful)
Experience with automation workflows on CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Helios, Terraform is a plus.
Create and document procedures, ServiceNow Knowledge Articles and Internal RBC Connect Web Pages related to the solutions we support


What you need to succeed?

Must Haves:


Advance experience with Visual Studio Or PowerShell Scripting
Advanced Excel experience (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
Strong SQL
Basic Python (can read/edit scripts; not expected to build complex systems)


Nice to Have:


Exposure to GitHub and Visual Studio Code
Willingness to use AI tools like GitHub Copilot
Knowledgeable with REST APIs and familiarity with Microsoft Graph API is a plus


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
A world-class training program in financial services
Flexible work/life balance options
Opportunities to do challenging work
Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
Opportunities to build close relationships with clients

Title: Data Analyst & Integrations Support I
Description:
You’re legendary. We’re hiring. Let’s talk!

Apply today for an opportunity to join one of North America’s top insurance premium financing companies. Since 1977, our company has grown to more than 500 Associates and 30 locations across the United States, Puerto Rico, and Canada.

Every day, we work to deliver on our Corporate Mission Statement: “To be the market leader by delivering innovative financial and technology solutions to help our Customers, partners, and investors achieve their goals.”

How do we do that? By empowering and encouraging our Associates to provide products and legendary Customer service unparalleled in our industry.

For Our Associates


Getting started: We offer a comprehensive paid training program that will make you feel prepared and excited about your role.
Staying healthy: Our Employee Assistance Program (for when you need confidential support) helps you keep an eye on short and long-term goals and any bumps that pop up along the way. We are also proud to offer subsidies to support your health and fitness goals by providing gym membership subsidies.
Registered Retirement Savings Plan: (RRSP) with matching employer contributions.
Company culture: Our Associates are the foundation of our company and we want you to enjoy working here! With company lunches, corporate outings, and even the occasional ice cream truck, you never know what will pop up to make your day.
Peace of mind: Our insurance benefits include medical, prescription, dental, vision, group term life, AD&D, private duty nursing, paramedical services, and much more!


All candidate screening and assessments are conducted by our team—AI is not used in the interview process.

Job Summary

The Data Analyst & Integrations Support role transforms data into actionable insights while ensuring the systems and integrations that support those insights operate reliably. This position develops and maintains Power BI reports and dashboards, performs ad hoc analyses, and translates complex data into clear visualizations for business stakeholders. In parallel, the role supports system integrations by maintaining data connections, monitoring APIs, and troubleshooting data or integration issues to minimize business disruption. The position partners cross-functionally with Operations, IT, development teams, and business leaders to monitor performance, identify trends, and ensure accurate, dependable data across platforms.

Key Responsibilities


Data Analytics:
Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI reports and dashboards for internal and external stakeholders
Create clear, actionable visualizations that communicate complex data insights to non-technical audiences
Design and implement self-service reporting solutions to enable data-driven decision making
Perform ad hoc analysis to answer business questions and support strategic initiatives
Write SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in data to provide actionable recommendations
Collect, clean, and prepare data for analysis and reporting
Maintain advanced proficiency in Power BI, SQL, and Excel
Apply mathematical and analytical skills for complex queries, data interpretation, and problem-solving
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand reporting needs and translate them into technical solutions
Ensure reports are accurate, timely, and aligned with business requirements
Integration Support:
Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve systems and integration issues, ensuring minimal downtime and impact on business operations
Create, monitor, and maintain API connections for integration between internal and external systems
Work closely with development teams, operations, sales, service, product owners, and third-party vendors to ensure systems meet the organization's business needs
Maintain detailed documentation of integration processes, configurations, and changes
Monitor and optimize integration performance, including load balancing, error handling, and process automation
Collaborate with external vendors and service providers to resolve integration-related issues and implement new solutions
Regularly monitor applications and systems in production to ensure they are running smoothly and meeting performance expectations
Perform product setup, user management, and portfolio maintenance
Ensure that incidents are resolved within defined SLAs, escalating issues when necessary to meet deadlines
Perform or facilitate root cause analysis for significant incidents to prevent recurrence and improve system performance
Recommend improvements to existing processes, tools, and workflows to optimize production support efficiency
Other duties as assigned

Qualifications


Minimum of 3–5 years of relevant experience in data analytics, reporting, and/or systems integration support roles.
Four-year college education: in Computer Science, MIS, Operations Research, Math, Finance, or related experience.
Proven experience in systems integration, middleware technologies, and APIs.
Data analysis and collection best practices
Ability to clearly communicate technical subjects and processes
Proficiency with Power BI (report and dashboard development)
Strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation
Advanced Excel skills
Strong knowledge of integration platforms and cloud-based technologies (e.g., AWS, Azure, PBS, Applied, Power Broker, Acturis, API, JSON, Postman).
Ability to communicate with all levels of staff/management in internal and external relationships
Strong analytical, reporting and problem-solving skills
Strong knowledge of math and logic aptitude
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.


Working Conditions


May require on-call support and availability outside of regular working hours to address urgent production issues.
Fast-paced work environment with tight deadlines and high priority on maintaining system uptime.


The annual compensation range for this position is $68,000 - $91,000 CAD.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Konrad is a next-generation digital consultancy. We are dedicated to solving complex business problems for our global clients with creative and forward-thinking solutions. Our employees enjoy a culture built on innovation and a commitment to creating best-in-class digital products in use by hundreds of millions of consumers around the world. We hire exceptionally smart, analytical, and hard-working people who are lifelong learners.

About The Role

This role sits on our consulting team, where your internal title will be Consultant, working primarily in a data analytics capacity across client engagements.

As a Consultant focused on Data Analytics, you are an early-to-mid career data professional with a strong foundation in analysis and a desire to grow both your technical and consulting skill set. Leveraging your analytics background, you will support the delivery of digital products for our clients through analytics and reporting solutions that help organizations better understand their data and make informed decisions.

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Who are we?

Cheil Worldwide is the 11th-largest creative agency in the world. Headquartered in Seoul, South Korea, Cheil was founded by Samsung in 1973 to grow its local and global business. We are driven by our passion to create mind-blowing ideas, technologies, and one-of-a-kind experiences by connecting advertising with digital; online and offline; data and events; and consumers and brands. Cheil North America has offices in Toronto, NYC and Dallas, and serves as the lead agency for all Samsung projects across the continent. Collectively, we are a unified force of creative problem-solvers who share a passion for developing ground-breaking ideas rooted in data, driven by insights, and enabled by innovative technology

Position Summary

Samsung.com is scaling its experimentation and insights practice. We need a data-driven analyst who can run A/B tests, govern data quality, and turn behavior patterns into clear, revenue-growing recommendations across every division (Mobile & Wearables, Home Entertainment, Digital Appliances, B2B).

Key Responsibilities


Plan, launch, and analyze A/B and multivariate tests in Adobe Target and Insider—defining hypotheses, sizing audiences, monitoring in-flight results, and presenting statistically sound read-outs.
Mine GA4, Adobe Analytics, and CDP datasets to uncover behavioral insights, segment performance (device, traffic source, campaign, division), and translate findings into prioritized optimization recommendations.
Design and maintain self-serve dashboards in Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI, automating data pipelines and enforcing metric definitions for stakeholders from squad level to executive.
Own Google Tag Manager configurations—creating events, enhanced-e-commerce tracking, and custom dimensions, performing end-to-end QA in dev and prod.
Define, document, and manage the GA4-compliant data layer that powers analytics, Adobe Target, and Insider tests, ensuring consistency, privacy compliance, and future scalability.
Collaborate cross-functionally with UX, Merchandising, Development, and Legal teams to ensure experimentation and tagging requirements are embedded in every release.
Champion data quality and governance by establishing validation checklists, monitoring discrepancies, and educating stakeholders on best practices.



Must-Have Qualifications


3–5 years in digital analytics, CRO, or experimentation for a high-traffic e-commerce site.
A/B testing experience with Adobe Target and/or Insider (or similar).
Advanced use of GA4 and Adobe Analytics—including explorations, calculated metrics, and segmentation.
Demonstrated data-layer management for GA4 (schema design, mapping, validation).
SQL/BigQuery proficiency and strong Excel/Sheets skills.
Dashboard development in Looker Studio / Tableau / Power BI.
Deep familiarity with GTM, tag-debugging tools, and browser/network debuggers.
Ability to explain statistical concepts (confidence, lift, p-values) and craft persuasive narratives for non-analysts.
Bridge the gap between artistic creative and hard performance data to ensure digital campaigns are built for conversion, not just engagement



DESIRED


Adobe RT-CDP Mastery: Experience leveraging Real-Time Customer Data Platforms to unify cross-channel data into actionable profiles for 1:1 personalization.
Omnichannel Personalization: Experience using Adobe Target or Insider to deliver personalized experiences across Web, Mobile (Samsung Members App), and CRM channels.



Nice-to-Have Extras


Server-side testing, Adobe Launch, or Tealium iQ experience.
Working knowledge of HTML/CSS/JavaScript for front-end troubleshooting.
Exposure to SEO, paid-media attribution, or product-recommendation engines.
Background in consumer electronics or retail e-commerce.



What Happens After You Apply

Application review: Our Human Resources Manager reviews every resume. We get upwards of 100+ applications for some roles, it can take a few days, if your resume is shortlisted, we'll be sure to reach out. We thank you for your patience!

Salary range: $70,000 - $75,000 CAD annually

Final compensation for this role will be determined by various factors such as a candidate’s relevant work experience, skills, and certifications

Work Arrangement:

This role is a fixed-term contract for a period of one (1) year, with the possibility of extension based on business needs and performance.

While this position follows a hybrid work model, with three (3) days in-office (Tuesday through Thursday) and two (2) days work-from-home (Monday and Friday), during business requirements, needs, client deadlines, you may be required to be in office on all 5 days.

Company Address: #100, 6790 Century Avenue, Mississauga, L5N 2V8

Title: Oakville Fire Data Analyst
Description:
Job Details

Permanent Full Time (CUPE 1329)

Posting Status

Open to all current Town of Oakville employees and external applicants

Closing Date

Applications for this position must be received at by no later than 11:59pm on May 31, 2026.

This job posting is for an existing vacancy and therefore will be filled accordingly.

Job Responsibilities

What gets measured, gets managed. The incumbent is a critical resource in our relentless pursuit of continuous improvement by ensuring the end-to-end journey is tracked, measured, and reported on. Key performance indicators that measure the health of service delivery efforts need to be consistently reported on and continuously improved upon as department requirements change.

Reporting to the Supervisor, Administrative Services, the data analyst is a key contributor to operational efficiency and effectiveness by analyzing complex structured and unstructured datasets to help guide business decisions. The incumbent will use a variety of analytical techniques to prepare, visualize, analyze, and communicate key performance measures that drive business decisions across the Oakville Fire Department, partner business units and various levels of government.

The incumbent will work in collaboration with the fire department leadership, operations teams, IT staff and external stakeholders to turn data into insights that can be used to make sound organizational business decisions and inform policy development.

The analyst provides core application data support for all supported departments by ensuring data is created and structured in a format that will provide management across the organization with insights to help in key decision making for their services.

Qualifications


Completion of a three-year Diploma or Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Information Management, or similar analytical disciplines.
A minimum of 3 years of experience in data analysis and reporting is an asset.
Experience soliciting and documenting client requirements and producing analyses, reports, and data visualizations based on the stated needs.
Experience using data to illuminate and solve problems.
Ability to communicate complex analysis in a clear manner for a variety of audiences.
Experience using data analysis tools such as Qlikview, MS Power BI, Google Analytics, Tableau, ArcGIS, FDM, First Due and Telestaff.
Ability to adapt to new software programs with administrative rights.
Experience working on data analytic projects in the areas of social networks, website analytics, with project performance measures and established KPI’s.
Strong understanding of compliance reporting to Emergency Management Ontario, Office of the Fire Marshal, and/or other legislative practices is considered an asset.
Experience working with project teams and all levels of staff.
Data Management Association (DAMA) Certified Data Management Professional (CDMP) or The Data Warehouse Institute (TDWI) Certified Business Intelligence Professional (CBIP) are considered an asset.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills, with the ability to understand complex inter-departmental processes to identify key performance measures.
Create and understand data structures, data management theories, principles and practices.
Strong understanding of the tasks associated with and challenges of working with unstructured data.
Competency with creation of data queries and data extraction methods and tools.
Ability to quickly understand end-user needs and requirements and the organizational goals and objectives.
Able to articulate complex data concepts to non-technical audiences.
Excellent oral and written communication, and presentation skills.
Direct experience and strong understanding of statistical research, data management, reporting, analysis, trends and forecasting.
Exceptional computer skills, including knowledge of Visio, SharePoint, Microsoft Office.
Knowledge of systems including Salesforce CRM, Mitel IVR, JDE Enterprise, Amanda, Google Analytics. ESRI GIS, and CityWorks is an asset.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Access and Excel including the design and maintenance of spreadsheets/databases/software programs.
Strong time-management skills.
Understanding of confidentiality requirements.
Strong team and customer-oriented focus.
Works independently, self-motivated and proactive.


DATED: May 11, 2026

The Town’s recruitment software includes elements of artificial intelligence to assist in the screening and short-listing of qualified candidates.

This job profile reflects the general requirements necessary to perform the principal functions of the job. This does not include all of the work requirements of the job. Applicants are required to demonstrate through their application and in the interview process that their qualifications match those specified. The minimum threshold score for the interview is 75%.

We thank all applicants and advise that only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Location: Vancouver, BC (Remote-First / Flexible Workspace)




Job Type: Full-Time




Please submit application through this link to be considered: https://forms.gle/fvM2xFCX1mza8n8P8




About Us

Skyora Ventures Inc. provides specialized data analytics services for a global Big Tech firm. We partner directly with their Real Estate and Workplace Services teams to transform enterprise-scale operational data into actionable insights. Our team handles massive datasets reflecting our client’s global EV charging networks, parking infrastructure, and workplace utilization metrics. By delivering deep analytics to their master planning teams, we help them optimize spatial efficiency, scale green utility infrastructure, and design data-driven workplaces at a global scale.




Role Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented, narrative-driven Data Analyst to champion our data initiatives and fuel strategic decision-making. In this role, you will do much more than build static spreadsheets- you will uncover the "story behind the numbers," pinpointing key operational trends, mapping anomalies, and delivering the "why" that helps guide our client's business forward.




Core Responsibilities

Data Synthesis: Extract, clean, and consolidate complex datasets from disparate enterprise sources to build a unified, highly accurate view of workplace operations.
Insight Generation: Analyze operational data and workplace utilization metrics to identify correlations, forecast trends, and support long-term project planning.
Ad-Hoc Analysis: Partner directly with leadership to execute deep-dive analyses on urgent business challenges and emerging strategic opportunities.
Process Improvement: Collaborate closely with operations teams to refine data collection methodologies, champion internal best practices, and safeguard data integrity.




Technical Requirements

SQL: Proficiency in writing clean, optimized queries to extract, manipulate, and model data from relational databases.
Spreadsheet Mastery: Advanced, expert-level fluency in Excel or Google Sheets (including Pivot Tables, nested formulas, arrays, and macros).
Data Visualization: Proven track record of building intuitive, high-impact dashboards that translate complex, technical data into clear visual narratives for business stakeholders.




The Ideal Candidate

Analytical & Strategic: You look far past surface-level numbers. If a key metric shifts by 10%, your first instinct is to dive into the data, isolate the root cause, and formulate actionable recommendations.
Meticulous: You bring a strict "measure twice, cut once" mindset to your workflow, ensuring rigorous data accuracy and quality control before insights reach leadership.
Strong Communicator: You effortlessly bridge the gap between technical metrics and business strategy, explaining complex analytical concepts clearly to diverse audiences.




Experience & Education

Experience: 3+ years of professional experience in a Data Analyst, Business Intelligence, or highly quantitative operational role.
Capabilities Over Credentials: We place immense value on proven capability, critical thinking, and a hands-on approach over formal degrees. If you possess a strong track record of delivering top-tier analytics, we want to see your work.
Work Authorization: Applicants must be legally entitled to work in Canada at the time of application.




How to Apply & Submission Requirements

To be considered for this position, please submit your resume alongside the following mandatory documentation via this link: https://forms.gle/fvM2xFCX1mza8n8P8




Required: A "One-Pager" Project Summary: A concise, one-page document detailing 1–2 data analysis projects you have owned. Please highlight your methodologies, your personal contributions, and the tangible business impact you delivered.




Please Note: This project summary is a core component of our vetting process to evaluate practical, real-world application. Applications submitted without this attached document will not be reviewed.




Compensation & Total Rewards

In compliance with the BC Pay Transparency Act, the base salary range for this position is:




Salary Range: $80,000 – $102,000 CAD annually. Final base salary within this bracket depends on specific qualifications, practical background, and technical assessment results.




Benefits & Perks:

Paid Time Off: 3 weeks (15 days) of paid annual vacation, plus 5 paid sick/medical days.
Retirement Security: RRSP matching program up to 3% available after one year of continuous employment.
Performance Bonus: Eligibility for an annual cash bonus based on individual performance and project milestones.




Skyora Ventures Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. We value a diverse workforce and are fully committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees under the BC Human Rights Code. Workplace and interview accommodations are available upon request for candidates participating in any phase of the recruitment process. In alignment with BC legislation, we maintain a strict anti-reprisal policy regarding wage discussions and do not request or consider past salary history.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Duties and Responsibilities are:


Collect, analyze, and interpret project, financial, and operational data from construction systems (ERP, project management, equipment, and safety).
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to monitor project performance, costs, schedules, productivity, and safety metrics.
Track key construction KPIs and provide actionable insights to support project planning, forecasting, and decision-making.
Extract, transform, and validate data obtained from various to build clean and consistent datasets for analysis.
Develop and maintain standardized data models and metrics that promote a single source of truth across teams.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across projects and reporting tools.
Automate reporting processes and support BI system implementation and optimization.
Collaborate with Project Managers, Finance, and Operations to meet data and reporting needs.
Monitor data quality and proactively identify inconsistencies or gaps in data integrity, working with technical teams to resolve issues.
Automate recurring data requests and streamline manual reporting processes.
Present insights clearly to management and assist teams in using dashboards effectively.
Stay updated on BI tools and construction analytics best practices to improve reporting and analysis.


Qualifications/Experience/Competencies


Completion of a bachelor’s degree in computer science/data science/information systems or related field
3+ years of experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst, Data Analyst, or similar role within a data-driven environment.
Experience with machine learning and analytics
Strong proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI
Experience with Google Sheets, including Google Apps Script for automation and integrations.
Familiarity with ETL concepts and data transformation tools
Comfortable working with APIs and cloud-based data platforms
Good understanding of data modeling, data governance, and best practices for maintaining data quality and consistency
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail and data accuracy.
Excellent communication skills; able to simplify complex data findings for non-technical audiences
Proactive, curious, and comfortable working both independently and collaboratively in a team setting
Organized, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a dynamic environment


Salary Range: $65,000 – $85,000 annually, depending on experience and qualifications

The Data Analyst is responsible for transforming data into actionable insights that drive business decisions. This role involves gathering, analyzing, and interpreting large datasets; developing dashboards and reports; and collaborating with cross-functional teams to support strategic planning and operational efficiency.

Competitive, experience‑based salary with annual pay reviews
Extended health benefits
RRSP matching up to 5% of base salary per year
Employee Stock Ownership Program – become a Vinci Construction group shareholder
3 weeks paid vacation starting Day 1 + paid holiday shutdown
Milestone awards to celebrate your achievements
Employee referral bonuses up to $2,000
Full training catalogue to support long‑term career growth
Maternity Leave Top‑Up (SUB Plan)
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for 24/7 personal support
Friday office snacks
Family‑friendly company events throughout the year
Free on‑site parking

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LayerZero

The Future is Omnichain.

Founded in 2021, LayerZero’s vision is to create a community of cross-chain developers, building dApps that are no longer constrained by individual blockchain capabilities. With LayerZero's simple, generic messaging protocol, builders will develop cross-chain dApps designed to unify the power of individual blockchains.

We are funded by the best investors in the world including:

a16z, Sequoia, PayPal, Binance Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Uniswap Labs, Circle Ventures, Delphi Digital, and many more.

About The Role

As we continue to grow at LayerZero, we're faced with an exciting set of challenges: more data, more developers, more teams, more projects, and more opportunities for innovation. To tackle these problems head-on, we're expanding our data team with a dedicated Data Analyst.

As a Data Analyst at LayerZero, you'll be at the forefront of shaping a rich data foundation for a company that's making a real impact in the web3 space. You'll work closely with teams and leaders to uncover insights, drive decision-making, and fuel our next-generation products and services.

What You'll Do

As a key member of the data team at LayerZero, you'll be diving headfirst into the world of crypto data - exploring on-chain wallets and contracts, block and transaction data, insights from in-house systems, and third-party intelligence. Your mission will be to combine these diverse datasets into rich, actionable data products for a broad group of stakeholders.


Leveraging and expanding our ever-growing Kimball dimensional model.
Writing SQL to create and expand insights in our in-house reporting solutions.
Collaborating with stakeholders across the organization to conduct ad-hoc explorations and analytics - from engineering questions to help understand production issues to product insights that illuminate the broader crypto landscape.
Be a key owner of data quality, building out insights that serve the data team itself to help guide our data efforts towards cleaner and richer data.
Compose pipelines by writing SQL code to clean, combine, refine, and aggregate data into the insights the organization needs.
Collaborating on new datasets to ingest into our Snowflake data warehouse, working closely with data engineers on your team.
Not afraid of pushing code that supports tens of billions of dollars in daily transaction volume.


Who You Are

We are looking for someone with previous data analyst experience. Likely with a bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics or related field, but we also consider and highly value equivalent practical experience.

Required


Strong SQL knowledge and experience
Proven track record in data modeling, statistics, and analytics
Experience working with a broad range of stakeholders
Strong convictions weakly held.
You enjoy working in a fast-paced start-up environment, where you'll need to take initiative and solve problems independently.
You understand the importance of data quality through incremental data development.


Nice to have


Experience with general programming, we use Python and Go on the data stack
Experience with Snowflake
Experience building DAG based data pipelines
Experience with streaming real-time data pipelines
Previous experience with blockchain technologies, smart contracts, and decentralized finance
Experience with Kimball dimensional modeling
Working on a mid-to-large scale data stacks


Equal Opportunity Employer

LayerZero Labs is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. LayerZero Labs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re searching for a Data Analyst. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for an Data Analyst to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $62,400- $78,000 with a 37.5-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Data Analyst, you’ll have the unique opportunity to lay the foundation for how Westland can leverage data to make important decisions.

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You strive to understand the data and love seeking ways to improve the quality of the data and reporting
You enjoy collaborating with others to identify and prioritize use cases for analysis and build out models to answer business questions
You love numbers and have a strong background in math, statistics or equivalent such as a bachelor’s degree in those areas or related fields
You can translate complex information and findings for business leaders
At least two years’ experience in a data analytics role
Previous experience with data analysis and modeling using SQL, R, and Python
Bonus: Experience in P&C insurance
Bonus: Experience using data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake or Microsoft Fabric Bonus: Experience with Git based version control on platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps


Once Here, You'll


Be a contributing member of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds.
Working with IT and others to design and improve the quality of our data and reporting by understanding current and future data analysis needs, business opportunities and risks.
Continuously seek out and identify areas to improve data reporting practices, data quality, and processes
Prepare and present findings to diverse audiences, including partners


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to drive data-driven insights and support product analytics for large-scale mobile applications and cloud-based services. This role will work cross-functionally to analyze user behavior, improve product performance, and help shape strategic decisions through advanced analytics and visualization.




Key Responsibilities

Design and develop data dashboards, reports, and data pipelines to support business insights and product analytics
Analyze large datasets to understand user behavior, product performance, and trends
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities to enhance customer experience and product innovation
Translate complex data into actionable insights for stakeholders
Work on complex analytical problems requiring in-depth evaluation of multiple variables
Apply appropriate analytical techniques and methodologies to deliver high-quality results
Leverage ML/AI tools to generate insights and improve reporting capabilities




Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, or related)
5+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or related field (preferably within a product-focused environment)
Advanced proficiency in SQL and working knowledge of Python
Strong experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau or similar)
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts and data modeling
Experience building and managing ETL processes and data pipelines (e.g., Airflow, Apache Spark, or similar)
Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS or GCP
Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively across teams




Nice to Have

Experience working with mobile or health-related data
Certifications in AWS, GCP, or data analytics
Exposure to or experience with machine learning or AI technologies

Title: Data Analyst II, Fraud
Description:
Job Description:

At Remitly, we believe everyone deserves the freedom to access, move, and manage their money wherever life takes them. Since 2011, we've tirelessly delivered on our promise to customers sending money globally, providing secure, simple, and reliable ways to manage their money, ensuring true peace of mind.

Whether it's supporting loved ones back home, growing a business across continents, or pursuing new opportunities abroad, we're not just here to move money— we're here to move our global customers forward.

We're looking for builders, reimaginers, and global thinkers who want to work at the intersection of technology, trust, and transformation. If that's you and you're ready to do the most meaningful work of your career—we invite you to join over 2,800 passionate Remitlians worldwide who are united by our vision to transform lives with trusted financial services that transcend borders

About the Role:

As a Data Analyst, Fraud, you will be part of Remitly's Identity & Trust Analytics Team. Your mission is to leverage analytical methods to identify, prevent, and mitigate fraud impacting our customers globally. You will develop a deep understanding of Remitly's fraud detection systems, regional risk profiles, and operational processes to improve fraud defenses while maintaining a seamless experience for legitimate customers. You'll partner with Product, Engineering, Business Management, and Compliance to customize and optimize fraud models and workflows, integrating data from legacy and new platforms. This hybrid role requires 2–3 days per week in the Vancouver office.

You Will:


Become an expert on Remitly's fraud data (e.g. chargebacks, promo abuse, scams, onboarding risk).
Build data-driven models and dashboards to analyze and monitor fraud exposure, balancing customer experience with operational efficiency.
Collect, normalize, and analyze data from legacy prebriefs, transaction logs, and onboarding systems to identify fraud trends and behavior anomalies.
Partner with Business Management and Product to continuously improve customer verification, KYC, KYB and compliance workflows.
Design and evaluate experiments and A/B tests to measure fraud control effectiveness and minimize false positives.
Develop and track risk metrics that quantify fraud exposure, policy compliance, and system performance.
Provide actionable insights and influence the development of fraud detection strategies, tools, and automation.


You Have:


3+ years in an analytics or fraud/risk analysis role within fintech, e-commerce, or digital payments.
Experience with fraud or trust analytics.
Bachelor's degree in mathematics, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline (or equivalent experience).
Proficiency with SQL and hands-on experience analyzing large datasets (Python, R, or statistical modeling experience preferred).
Strong understanding of fraud taxonomies (chargebacks, identity theft, scams, onboarding risk, and promo abuse).
Demonstrated ability to distill business problems, develop hypotheses, perform analysis, and communicate clear recommendations.
Excellent data storytelling and communication skills; ability to translate complex findings into business decisions.


Compensation Details. The starting base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $125000. In Canada, Remitly employees are shareholders in our Company and equity is part of our total compensation plan. Your recruiter can share more information about medical benefits offered, as well as other financial benefits and total compensation components offered with this role.

Our Benefits


Four weeks vacation
Health Benefits
Mental Health & Family Forming Benefits
RRSP plan with company match
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
Life Insurance & Disability
Continuing education and travel benefits


Our Connected Work Culture: Driving Innovation, Together

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Title: Product Integrity Analyst
Description:
Requirements

Description & Requirements

who are we

lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people.

About This Team

Quality is at the heart of who we are. The Product Integrity Data Analytics and Reporting team designs and operates the global product integrity reporting framework and ecosystem to maximize product value for guests while minimizing risk to the brand. The team serves as one of the pillars at the centre of excellence for policy, data, systems, and documentation, partnering closely with regional Product Integrity teams across markets.

What You’ll Do

The Data Analyst is an experienced individual contributor who helps solves ambiguous business problems through analytics, systems thinking, and strong cross‑functional collaboration. Operating as a data- and systems-enabled, AI-based local support and consulting function for Product Integrity, this role prepares, validates, and analyzes data to support dashboards, recurring reporting, and ad-hoc analyses in partnership with Senior Analysts to answer Product Integrity business questions. This role supports the org by partnering closely with technology, product management, e‑commerce, retail, and product and supply chain functions from design and development through sourcing, fulfillment, merchandising, planning, and allocation. The Data Analyst contributes to foundational data models, improves reporting processes and documentation, and independently delivers smaller analytical projects with clear data-driven insights. The role contributes to the development and maintenance of analytical reporting frameworks by maintaining Power BI dashboards, executing data exports across multiple systems, and troubleshooting data or refresh issues. The Data Analyst helps in framing problem statements, synthesizing insights across multiple data sources, and driving actionable recommendations while aligning with established Product Integrity strategies, standards, and systems.

Core Responsibilities


Support regular refresh, monitoring, and maintenance of Power BI reports, including investigating refresh failures and technical issues.
Assist with Power BI reporting business-driven enhancements and automation efforts
Contribute to executive-level program reporting deliverables, including manual data extraction and preparing weekly and quarterly reporting outputs for Product Integrity programs (Product Voice of Guest, Voice of Process, etc.)
Deliver ad-hoc data requests, by synthesizing data across siloed systems and teams to identify trends, highlight key insights, and help drive actionable recommendations.
Execute scheduled data exports and uploads to SharePoint, Smartsheet and reporting tools, ensuring timely distribution to cross‑functional partners and stakeholders.
Assist with day-to-day operation workflows, including ServiceNow ticket assignment to team members or cross-functional partners and coordination of Planner tasks.
Maintaining reporting access and documentation, including PVOG dashboard access requests and updating system access guide as changes or gaps are identified.
Support Supplier analytics data troubleshooting, assisting with issue resolution and performing routine data updates to ensure data accuracy.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.
Minimum 2 years of experience in data analytics and reporting; retail, apparel, or supply chain experience preferred.
Expert‑level proficiency in Excel, Power BI, SQL, data modeling, and database structures; experience with Smartsheet.
Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and systems‑thinking capabilities.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present insights to stakeholders at all levels.
Proven ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and navigate ambiguity.


must haves


Acknowledge the presence of choice in every moment and take personal responsibility for your life.
Possess an entrepreneurial spirit and continuously innovate to achieve great results.
Communicate with honesty and kindness and create the space for others to do the same.
Lead with courage, knowing the possibility of greatness is bigger than the fear of failure.
Foster connection by putting people first and building trusting relationships.
Integrate fun and joy as a way of being and working, aka doesn’t take yourself too seriously.


Additional Notes

Authorization to work in Canada is required for this role.

Compensation And Benefits Package

lululemon’s compensation offerings are grounded in a pay-for-performance philosophy that recognizes exceptional individual and team performance. The typical hiring range for this position is from $71,400 - $93,800 CAD annually; the base pay offered is based on market location and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and internal equity. As part of our total rewards offering, permanent employees in this position may be eligible for our competitive annual bonus program, subject to program eligibility requirements.  

At Lululemon, Investing In Our People Is a Top Priority. We Believe That When Life Works, Work Works. We Strive To Be The Place Where Inclusive Leaders Come To Develop And Enable All To Be Well. Recognizing Our Teams For Their Performance And Dedication, Other Components Of Our Total Rewards Offerings Include Support Of Career Development, Wellbeing, And Personal Growth


Extended health and dental benefits, and mental health plans
Paid time off
Savings and retirement plan matching
Generous employee discount
Fitness & yoga classes
Parenthood top-up
Extensive catalog of development course offerings
People networks, mentorship programs, and leadership series (to name a few)


Note: The incentive programs, benefits, and perks have certain eligibility requirements. The Company reserves the right to alter these incentive programs, benefits, and perks in whole or in part at any time without advance notice.

workplace arrangement

This role is classified as Hybrid under our SSC Workplace Policy:


Hybrid; In-person collaboration is important, and much of the role can be performed remotely. Work is performed onsite at least 4 days per week.

Title: Data Analyst - FTT
Description:
Marketing Statement

A career at TransLink and our family of companies means working with people with a wide range of skills and perspectives, all teaming up towards a common goal: preserving and enhancing the region's world-envied quality of life. Together, we connect the region and enhance its livability by providing a sustainable transit and transportation network, embraced by our communities and people.

At TransLink we are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable and accessible workplace, recognizing the unique value and skills every individual brings.

Looking for a great place to work where your contributions are valued and you can make a difference in a vibrant city? At TransLink, one of BC’s Top Employers, you'll help make Metro Vancouver a better place to live, built on transportation excellence. Put your future in motion!

Responsibilities

Performs data analysis of Enterprise data and provides expertise and advice to business and BTS stakeholders on the application of Data Management practices and principles. Supports the Enterprise Information Governance program. Performs detailed analyses of business areas to identify, understand, model and define data, business data flows and processes in conjunction with business and BTS stakeholders. Identifies data anomalies, patterns, overlaps and duplications and recommends improvements according to data management best practices and TransLink standards. Develops and implements data quality control and monitoring solutions to support data quality for the enterprise. Works with application support teams, the Data Warehouse team and system development project teams to offer data management expertise and services. Creates and maintains the conceptual, logical and physical data models that form the Enterprise data architecture and updates the corporate metadata repository, ensuring the integrity and consistent definition and use of Enterprise data. Keeps up to date with new developments in data management practices and technologies to support and improve data management services. Installs, maintains and administers tools and applications owned by the Data Management Team.

Education

Qualifications

Diploma in computer systems technology, with emphasis on database design, data modeling, systems analysis, system development methodologies and the use of C.A.S.E. tools.

Experience

Requires three (3) years of previous experience in an environment where logical/physical data modeling, data analysis techniques, data management and business analysis skills have been demonstrated. Requires a further period of up to one (1) year in the position to become familiar with TransLink’s business operations and environment and the business requirements for data and information.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

This is a full-time temporary position for 12 months.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This positions offers the ability to work predominately in a remote capacity within B.C. The business will request onsite presence at times for operational reasons.

Rate of Pay

Salary $8,236 - $9,896 per month (Actual salary offered will be commensurate with education, experience and internal parity).

The Total Compensation Package includes Extended Health, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan upon eligibility. Focus on your development through training, and mentorship programs. Enjoy a variety of health and wellness programs, including access to gym facilities. Speak to us to know more about what we offer.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner of the page or go to http://www.translink.ca/careers to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: March 10, 2026

Closing Date: Open until filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

TransLink is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca.
